```{r figure 2 stats}
    end_wave = factor(character(), levels = wave_levels),
    cluster = character(),
    cluster_disp = factor(character(), levels = c("Lower-Risk","Higher-Risk")),
    cumrisk = double(),
    cumrisk_lo = double(),
    cumrisk_hi = double())
}

#F2.2.1 If cumulative-risk CIs are absent in the exported table, backfill from saved glm fits
need_cum_ci <- (
  nrow(cum_by_wave) == 0 ||
    !"cumrisk_lo" %in% names(cum_by_wave) ||
    all(is.na(cum_by_wave$cumrisk_lo)))

if (need_cum_ci) {
  cum_ci_list <- lapply(OUT_FIG, function(out) {
    fit <- fits_primary[[out]]
    if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
    
    dat <- bd_pp_model %>%
      dplyr::filter(outcome == out) %>%
      droplevels() %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        end_wave = factor(end_wave, levels = wave_levels),
        cluster = factor(cluster))
    
    if (!nrow(dat)) return(NULL)
    
    cumrisk_by_wave_ci_mvn_glm(fit, dat, B = 2000) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        outcome = out,
        model = "logit",
        end_wave = factor(end_wave, levels = wave_levels),
        cluster_disp = factor(coerce_cluster(cluster), levels = names(cluster_cols_f2))) %>%
      dplyr::select(outcome, model, end_wave, cluster, cluster_disp, cumrisk, cumrisk_lo, cumrisk_hi)
  })
  
  cum_by_wave <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::compact(cum_ci_list))
}

#F2.3 Prepare age median labels
#F2.3.1 Compute median ages per wave in the model data
age_meds_tbl <- bd_pp_model %>%
  dplyr::group_by(end_wave) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(age_med = median(age_mid, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(end_wave = as.character(end_wave))

#F2.3.2 Create named vector of median ages
age_meds_vec <- setNames(age_meds_tbl$age_med, age_meds_tbl$end_wave)

#F2.3.3 Create x-axis labels with median ages
x_lab_vec <- setNames(
  ifelse(is.finite(age_meds_vec[wave_levels]),
         sprintf("%s\nMedian Mid-Interval Age=%.1f", wave_pretty[wave_levels], age_meds_vec[wave_levels]),
         sprintf("%s\nMedian Mid-Interval Age=-", wave_pretty[wave_levels])),
  wave_levels)

#F2.4 Compute empirical hazards and cumulative incidence by wave and cluster
#F2.4.1 Empirical hazards by wave and cluster
emp_cum_by_wave <- bd_pp_model %>%
  dplyr::filter(outcome %in% OUT_FIG) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    cluster_disp = factor(coerce_cluster(cluster), levels = names(cluster_cols_f2)),
    end_wave = factor(end_wave, levels = wave_levels)) %>%
  
  #F2.4.1.1 Per-interval empirical hazard among those at risk
  dplyr::group_by(outcome, cluster_disp, end_wave) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(h = mean(event == 1L, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  
  #F2.4.1.2 Roll up to cumulative incidence: 1 - Π(1 - h_j)
  dplyr::group_by(outcome, cluster_disp) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(end_wave, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(cumrisk_obs = 1 - cumprod(1 - pmin(pmax(h, 0), 1))) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

#F2.5 Plot predicted and observed hazard by wave and cluster
plot_haz_wave <- function(df, out_key, panel_letter = NULL) {
  
  d <- df %>%
    dplyr::filter(outcome == out_key, model == "logit") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      end_wave = factor(as.character(end_wave), levels = wave_levels),
      cluster_disp = factor(cluster_disp, levels = names(cluster_cols_f2))) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(cluster_disp, end_wave) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(cluster_disp), is.finite(hazard))
  
  if (is.null(panel_letter)) {
    i <- match(out_key, OUT_FIG)
    panel_letter <- LETTERS[i]
  }
  
  has_ci <- nrow(d) > 0 &&
    all(c("lo", "hi") %in% names(d)) &&
    any(is.finite(d$lo) & is.finite(d$hi), na.rm = TRUE)
  
  emp <- emp_cum_by_wave %>%
    dplyr::filter(outcome == out_key) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      end_wave = factor(as.character(end_wave), levels = wave_levels),
      cluster_disp = factor(cluster_disp, levels = names(cluster_cols_f2)))
  
  ggplot() +
    
    #F2.5.1 Add hazard CI ribbon behind the model-estimated lines
    { if (has_ci) geom_ribbon(
      data = d,
      aes(
        x = end_wave,
        ymin = lo,
        ymax = hi,
        fill = cluster_disp,
        group = cluster_disp),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      alpha = 0.15,
      color = NA) } +
    geom_line(
      data = d,
      aes(x = end_wave, y = hazard, color = cluster_disp, group = cluster_disp),
      linewidth = 0.7,
      na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_point(
      data = d,
      aes(x = end_wave, y = hazard, color = cluster_disp),
      size = 1.4,
      na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_point(
      data = emp,
      aes(x = end_wave, y = h, color = cluster_disp),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      shape = 1,
      size = 1.6) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = x_lab_vec, drop = FALSE) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.1)) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = cluster_cols_f2,
      breaks = names(cluster_cols_f2),
      labels = names(cluster_cols_f2),
      drop = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = cluster_cols_f2,
      breaks = names(cluster_cols_f2),
      labels = names(cluster_cols_f2),
      guide = "none",
      drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("(", panel_letter, ")  ", OUT_LABS[[out_key]], " Interval Hazard"),
      x = "Wave (Interval End)",
      y = "First-Onset Hazard",
      color = "Cluster") +
    axes_only_theme()
}

#F2.6 Plot cumulative incidence by wave and cluster
plot_cum_wave <- function(df, out_key) {
  d <- df %>%
    dplyr::filter(.data$outcome == out_key) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      end_wave = factor(as.character(end_wave), levels = wave_levels),
      cluster_disp = factor(cluster_disp, levels = names(cluster_cols_f2)))
  has_ci <- all(c("cumrisk_lo","cumrisk_hi") %in% names(d)) &&
    any(is.finite(d$cumrisk_lo) & is.finite(d$cumrisk_hi), na.rm = TRUE)
  i <- match(out_key, OUT_FIG)
  panel_letter <- LETTERS[i + 4]
  
  #F2.6.1 Observed cumulative for each outcome
  emp <- emp_cum_by_wave %>%
    dplyr::filter(outcome == out_key) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      end_wave = factor(as.character(end_wave), levels = wave_levels),
      cluster_disp = factor(cluster_disp, levels = names(cluster_cols_f2)))
  
  #F2.6.2 Plot cumulative incidence by wave and cluster
  ggplot(d, aes(x = end_wave, y = cumrisk, color = cluster_disp, group = cluster_disp)) +
    { if (has_ci) geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = cumrisk_lo, ymax = cumrisk_hi, fill = cluster_disp),
                              alpha = 0.15, color = NA) } +
    geom_line(size = 0.9) +
    geom_point(size = 1.8) +
    
    #F2.6.3 Overlay observed empirical cumulative incidence
    geom_point(data = emp,
               aes(x = end_wave, y = cumrisk_obs, color = cluster_disp),
               shape = 1, size = 1.8, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    
    #F2.6.4 Scales and labels
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.1), limits = c(0, 0.30)) +
    scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE, labels = x_lab_vec) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = cluster_cols_f2,
      breaks = names(cluster_cols_f2),
      labels = names(cluster_cols_f2),
      drop = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = cluster_cols_f2,
      breaks = names(cluster_cols_f2),
      labels = names(cluster_cols_f2),
      guide = "none",
      drop = FALSE) +
    labs(title = paste0("(", panel_letter, ")  ", OUT_LABS[[out_key]], " Cumulative Incidence"),
         x = "Wave (Interval End)", y = "Cumulative Risk", color = "Cluster") +
    axes_only_theme()
}

#F2.7 Build combined by wave and cumulative plot panels
#F2.7.1 Generate hazard and cumulative panels for each outcome
haz_panels <- lapply(seq_along(OUT_FIG), function(i) {
  plot_haz_wave(haz_by_wave, OUT_FIG[i], panel_letter = LETTERS[i])
})
cum_panels <- lapply(OUT_FIG, function(o) plot_cum_wave(cum_by_wave, o))

#F2.7.2 Prepare the panel versions used in the final layout
haz_layout_panels <- list(
  haz_panels[[1]] + strip_x,
  haz_panels[[2]] + strip_x,
  haz_panels[[3]],
  haz_panels[[4]]
)

cum_layout_panels <- list(
  cum_panels[[1]] + strip_x,
  cum_panels[[2]] + strip_x,
  cum_panels[[3]],
  cum_panels[[4]]
)

#F2.7.3 Extract a shared legend with explicit white background
legend_f2 <- cowplot::get_legend(
  haz_panels[[1]] +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA))
)

#F2.7.4 Remove legends from the actual panel plots
haz_layout_panels <- lapply(haz_layout_panels, function(p) p + theme(legend.position = "none"))
cum_layout_panels <- lapply(cum_layout_panels, function(p) p + theme(legend.position = "none"))

#F2.7.5 Align all 8 panels simultaneously so the top and bottom blocks share identical geometry
aligned_f2 <- cowplot::align_plots(
  plotlist = c(haz_layout_panels, cum_layout_panels),
  align = "hv",
  axis = "tblr",
  greedy = FALSE
)

#F2.7.6 Rebuild the top and bottom 2x2 grids from the already-aligned grobs
haz_grid <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  plotlist = aligned_f2[1:4],
  ncol = 2,
  rel_widths = c(1, 1),
  rel_heights = c(1, 1))

cum_grid <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  plotlist = aligned_f2[5:8],
  ncol = 2,
  rel_widths = c(1, 1),
  rel_heights = c(1, 1))

#F2.7.7 Stack the two aligned grids
fig2_main <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  haz_grid,
  cum_grid,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 1),
  align = "v",
  axis = "lr")

#F2.7.8 Wrap the legend in a white drawing surface
legend_plot_f2 <- cowplot::ggdraw() +
  cowplot::draw_grob(legend_f2, x = 0, y = 0, width = 1, height = 1) +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA))

#F2.8 Combine figure + legend
fig2 <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  fig2_main,
  legend_plot_f2,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 0.08))

print(fig2)

#F2.9 Save Figure 2 with white background (avoid transparent legend band artifacts)
ggplot2::ggsave(filename = file.path(fig_dir, "Figure_02_DTH_Hazards_and_Cumulative_by_Cluster.pdf"), plot = fig2, width = 9.5, height = 11, bg = "white")

ggplot2::ggsave(filename = file.path(fig_dir, "Figure_02_DTH_Hazards_and_Cumulative_by_Cluster.png"), plot = fig2, width = 9.5, height = 11, dpi = 720, bg = "white")


## In Line Stats Related to BD DTH Analyses (Figure 2) ##

#F2.10 Helper functions for in-line stats
#F2.10.1 Create reference frame for predictions from fitted primary glm model
make_ref_frame <- function(fit, dat) {
    make_ref_frame_glm(fit = fit, dat = dat)
}

#F2.10.2 Cumulative risk from hazards helper
cumrisk_from_haz <- function(h, k) {
  h <- pmin(pmax(h, 1e-8), 1 - 1e-8)
  1 - exp(sum(log1p(-h[seq_len(k)])))
}

#F2.10.3 Wald CI helper
wald_ci <- function(theta_hat, grad, V, level = 0.95, log_scale = FALSE) {
  
  #F2.10.3.1 Small ridge in case of near-singular V
  if (any(!is.finite(V))) V[!is.finite(V)] <- 0
  V <- V + diag(1e-10, nrow(V))
  se <- sqrt(drop(t(grad) %*% V %*% grad))
  z  <- qnorm((1 + level)/2)
  if (log_scale) {
    lo <- theta_hat * exp(-z * se)
    hi <- theta_hat * exp(+z * se)
  } else {
    lo <- theta_hat - z * se
    hi <- theta_hat + z * se
  }
  list(se = se, lo = lo, hi = hi, z = theta_hat / se, p = 2 * pnorm(-abs(theta_hat / se)))
}

#F2.10.4 Year-6 cumulative risk difference and ratio from primary glm hazards
year6_rd_rr_delta <- function(fit, dat, wave_key = "ses-06A") {
  obj <- make_ref_frame(fit, dat)
  ref <- obj$ref; X <- obj$X; beta <- obj$beta; V <- obj$V
  waves <- obj$waves

  if (!wave_key %in% waves) wave_key <- tail(waves, 1)
  k_idx <- match(wave_key, waves)

  #F2.10.4.1 Map C1/C2 -> Higher/Lower using coerce_cluster()
  cl_levels <- levels(ref$cluster)
  disp <- coerce_cluster(cl_levels)
  role <- setNames(rep(NA_character_, length(cl_levels)), cl_levels)
  role[disp == "Higher-Risk"] <- "HR"
  role[disp == "Lower-Risk"] <- "LR"
  if (anyNA(role)) {
    
    #F2.10.4.2 Fallback: choose HR as the one with larger point CR
    point_cr <- sapply(cl_levels, function(cl) {
      rows <- which(ref$cluster == cl)
      h <- plogis(as.numeric(X[rows, , drop = FALSE] %*% beta))
      cumrisk_from_haz(h, k_idx)
    })
    role[] <- "LR"; role[names(which.max(point_cr))] <- "HR"
  }

  #F2.10.4.3 Functions to compute cumulative risk by cluster
  make_f <- function(cl) {
    rows <- which(ref$cluster == cl)
    function(b) {
      h <- plogis(as.numeric(X[rows, , drop = FALSE] %*% b))
      cumrisk_from_haz(h, k_idx)
    }
  }

  #F2.10.4.4 Cumulative risks, RD, RR, and their CIs
  f_HR <- make_f(names(role)[role == "HR"])
  f_LR <- make_f(names(role)[role == "LR"])
  CR_HR <- f_HR(beta); CR_LR <- f_LR(beta)
  CR_HR <- pmin(pmax(CR_HR, 1e-8), 1 - 1e-8)
  CR_LR <- pmin(pmax(CR_LR, 1e-8), 1 - 1e-8)
  RD <- CR_HR - CR_LR
  RR <- CR_HR / CR_LR
  g_HR <- numDeriv::grad(f_HR, beta)
  g_LR <- numDeriv::grad(f_LR, beta)
  g_RD  <- g_HR - g_LR
  ci_RD <- wald_ci(RD, g_RD, V, level = 0.95, log_scale = FALSE)

  #F2.10.4.5 Log-RR & CI
  g_logRR  <- g_HR / CR_HR - g_LR / CR_LR
  se_logRR <- sqrt(drop(t(g_logRR) %*% V %*% g_logRR))
  z <- qnorm(0.975)
  RR_lo <- RR * exp(-z * se_logRR)
  RR_hi <- RR * exp(+z * se_logRR)
  p_logRR <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(log(RR) / se_logRR))

  #F2.10.4.6 Return results
  list(
    wave = wave_key,
    CR_HR = CR_HR, CR_LR = CR_LR,
    RD = RD, RD_lo = ci_RD$lo, RD_hi = ci_RD$hi, RD_p = ci_RD$p,
    RR = RR, RR_lo = RR_lo, RR_hi = RR_hi, RR_p = p_logRR)
}

#F2.11 Compute Year-6 cumulative risk differences and ratios by outcome and store in a table
cum6 <- lapply(OUT_FIG, function(out) {
  fit <- fits_primary[[out]]; if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  dat <- bd_pp_model %>%
    dplyr::filter(outcome == out) %>%
    droplevels() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
        
        #F2.11.1 Ensure factors have correct levels
        end_wave = factor(end_wave, levels = wave_levels),
        cluster = factor(cluster),
        sex = factor(sex),
        site_factor = factor(site_factor))

  #F2.11.2 Compute Year-6 cumulative risk RD/RR
  res <- year6_rd_rr_delta(fit, dat, wave_key = "ses-06A")
  tibble::tibble(
    outcome = out,
    out_lab = OUT_LABS[out],
    wave = res$wave,
    HR = res$CR_HR, LR = res$CR_LR,
    RD = res$RD, RD_lo = res$RD_lo, RD_hi = res$RD_hi, RD_p = res$RD_p,
    RR = res$RR, RR_lo = res$RR_lo, RR_hi = res$RR_hi, RR_p = res$RR_p)
    }) %>% 
    dplyr::bind_rows()

#F2.12 Clean output table for in-line reporting
cum6_lines <- cum6 %>%
  mutate(
    line = glue::glue(
      "{out_lab} Year-6 cumulative risk: ",
      "{scales::percent(HR, 0.1)} (HR) vs {scales::percent(LR, 0.1)} (LR); ",
      "RD = {scales::percent(RD, 0.1)} [{scales::percent(RD_lo, 0.1)}, {scales::percent(RD_hi, 0.1)}], p = {sprintf('%.3f', RD_p)}; ",
      "RR = {sprintf('%.2f', RR)} [{sprintf('%.2f', RR_lo)}, {sprintf('%.2f', RR_hi)}], p = {sprintf('%.3f', RR_p)}")) %>% 
      pull(line)

#F2.13 Print cumulative risk difference/ratio lines
cat(paste("•", cum6_lines), sep = "\n")

#F2.14 Extract cluster and age effects for in-line reporting
#F2.14.1 Create a robust outcome-key helper for DTH summary tables
to_bd_outcome_key <- function(x) {
  key <- gsub("[^a-z0-9]", "", tolower(as.character(x)))

  dplyr::case_when(
    key %in% c("bdi", "bipolari") ~ "bipolar_I",
    key %in% c("bdii", "bipolarii") ~ "bipolar_II",
    key %in% c("bdnos", "bipolarnos") ~ "bd_nos",
    key %in% c("bsd", "anybsd", "anybipolarspectrumdisorder") ~ "any_bsd",
    TRUE ~ as.character(x))
}

#F2.14.2 Create a robust outcome-label helper for DTH summary tables
to_bd_out_lab <- function(x) {
  key <- to_bd_outcome_key(x)

  dplyr::case_when(
    key == "bipolar_I" ~ "BD-I",
    key == "bipolar_II" ~ "BD-II",
    key == "bd_nos" ~ "BD-NOS",
    key == "any_bsd" ~ "Any BSD",
    TRUE ~ as.character(x))
}

#F2.14.3 Create a helper to format p-values for inline text
fmt_p_inline <- function(p) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(p) ~ "-",
    p < .001 ~ "< .001",
    TRUE ~ paste0("= ", sprintf("%.3f", p)))
}

#F2.14.4 Determine whether the robust SE column is named se_method or se_type
se_col <- if ("se_method" %in% names(bd_dth_sum)) {
  "se_method"
} else if ("se_type" %in% names(bd_dth_sum)) {
  "se_type"
} else {
  stop("Could not find either 'se_method' or 'se_type' in bd_dth_sum.")
}

#F2.15 Primary cluster main effect = adjusted pooled interval-onset OR (Higher- vs Lower-Risk)
cluster_effects_primary <- bd_dth_sum %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    model == "logit_primary",
    .data[[se_col]] == "CR2",
    grepl("^cluster", term),
    !grepl(":", term)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    outcome_key = to_bd_outcome_key(outcome),
    out_lab = to_bd_out_lab(outcome),
    OR = dplyr::coalesce(OR, exp(estimate)),
    OR_lo = dplyr::coalesce(ci_lo, exp(estimate - 1.96 * se)),
    OR_hi = dplyr::coalesce(ci_hi, exp(estimate + 1.96 * se)),
    pv = p.value) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(match(outcome_key, OUT_FIG))

cluster_lines_primary <- cluster_effects_primary %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    line = glue::glue(
      "{out_lab}: primary adjusted Higher- vs Lower-Risk cluster OR = ",
      "{sprintf('%.2f', OR)} [{sprintf('%.2f', OR_lo)}, {sprintf('%.2f', OR_hi)}], ",
      "p {fmt_p_inline(pv)}.")) %>%
  dplyr::pull(line)

if (length(cluster_lines_primary) > 0) {
  cat(paste("•", cluster_lines_primary), sep = "\n")
  cat("\n")
}

#F2.16 Primary age term = between-person age only
age_effects_primary <- bd_dth_sum %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    model == "logit_primary",
    .data[[se_col]] == "CR2",
    term == "age_mid_between") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    outcome_key = to_bd_outcome_key(outcome),
    out_lab = to_bd_out_lab(outcome),
    OR = dplyr::coalesce(OR, exp(estimate)),
    pv = p.value,
    lbl = "between-person age") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(match(outcome_key, OUT_FIG))

age_lines_primary <- age_effects_primary %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    line = glue::glue(
      "{out_lab}: primary {lbl} OR = {sprintf('%.2f', OR)} ",
      "(p {fmt_p_inline(pv)}).")) %>%
  dplyr::pull(line)

if (length(age_lines_primary) > 0) {
  cat(paste("•", age_lines_primary), sep = "\n")
}

#F2.16.1 Sensitivity note for within-person orthogonalized age
age_effects_within <- bd_dth_sum %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    model == "logit_within_orth",
    .data[[se_col]] == "CR2",
    term == "age_mid_cwc_orth") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    outcome_key = to_bd_outcome_key(outcome),
    out_lab = to_bd_out_lab(outcome),
    OR = dplyr::coalesce(OR, exp(estimate)),
    pv = p.value,
    lbl = "within-person age (orthogonalized sensitivity)") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(match(outcome_key, OUT_FIG))

if (nrow(age_effects_within) > 0) {
  age_lines_within <- age_effects_within %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      line = glue::glue(
        "{out_lab}: sensitivity {lbl} OR = {sprintf('%.2f', OR)} ",
        "(p {fmt_p_inline(pv)}).")) %>%
    dplyr::pull(line)

  cat("\n")
  cat(paste("•", age_lines_within), sep = "\n")
}

```

## Table S2: Characteristics of Participants in the Prevalence of Bipolar Disorders Over Time by Risk Cluster Analysis

- Purpose: Describe the GEE prevalence sample by wave and cluster; report demographics and occurrence n (%) per outcome and totals
- Inputs: bd_panel (panel format) with status, age, sex, site, cluster, wave, outcome; produces bd_panel_model via model-ready filters
- Outputs: Saves Table_S2_Prevalence_Characteristics_by_Cluster as HTML/PNG/RTF; prints a gt table
- Notes: Denominator for occurrence rows excludes missing status; demographics taken from Any BSD panel per wave; clusters displayed as Higher-/Lower-Risk

```{r table s2}

## Table S2 - Prevalence (GEE) analysis characteristics ##

#S2.1 Prepare the BD panel dataset
#S2.1.1 Coerce key variables
bd_panel <- bd_panel %>%
  mutate(
    participant_id = as.character(participant_id),
    family_id = factor(as.character(family_id)),
    site_factor = factor(site_factor),
    cluster = factor(cluster),
    wave = factor(wave),
    outcome = factor(outcome),
    sex = factor(sex))

#S2.1.2 Require outcome responses to exist and be 0/1
bd_panel <- bd_panel %>% mutate("status" := as.integer(.data[["status"]]))

#S2.1.3 Model-ready filter (post-QC) data used for BD prevalence analyses
bd_panel_model <- bd_panel %>%
  filter(.data[["status"]] %in% c(0L,1L)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(cluster),
    !is.na(sex),
    !is.na(site_factor),
    !is.na(family_id),
    !is.na(wave)) %>%
  
  #S2.1.3.1 Require age fields present for prediction/interpretation
  filter(!is.na(age_wave), !is.na(age_wave_cwc), !is.na(age_wave_between))

#S2.1.4 Set relevant reference levels of factor variables
bd_panel_model <- bd_panel_model %>%
  mutate(
    cluster = forcats::fct_relevel(cluster, "C2"),
    wave = forcats::fct_relevel(wave, "ses-02A", "ses-04A", "ses-06A"),
    sex = forcats::fct_relevel(sex, "Female"),
    site_factor = forcats::fct_relevel(site_factor, "site01"))

#S2.1.5 Guards
#S2.1.5.1 Guard: ensure bd_panel_model exists and is a data frame
stopifnot(exists("bd_panel_model"), is.data.frame(bd_panel_model))

#S2.1.5.2 Guard: response var (default to "status" if params$response_var not present)
status_var <- if (exists("params") && !is.null(params$response_var)) params$response_var else "status"
stopifnot(status_var %in% names(bd_panel_model))

#S2.1.5.3 Again force outcomes to {0,1,NA}
bd_panel_model[[status_var]] <- as.integer(bd_panel_model[[status_var]])
bd_panel_model[[status_var]][!bd_panel_model[[status_var]] %in% c(0L,1L)] <- NA_integer_

#S2.1.6 Helper functions for Table S2
#S2.1.6.1 Set wave -> time labels (2y/4y/6y)
time_map <- c("ses-02A" = "2y", "ses-04A" = "4y", "ses-06A" = "6y")
time_levels <- c("2y","4y","6y")

#S2.1.6.2 Formatting helpers for descriptive stats
fmt_mean_sd <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (!length(x)) return("\u2014")
  sprintf("%.2f \u00B1 %.2f", mean(x), stats::sd(x))
}
fmt_n <- function(n) ifelse(is.na(n), "\u2014", format(as.integer(n), big.mark = ","))
fmt_n_pct <- function(n, den) {
  out <- ifelse(is.na(den) | den == 0,
    "0 (0.0%)",
    sprintf("%s (%.1f%%)", fmt_n(n), 100 * n / den))
  as.character(out)
}

#S2.1.7 Prepare panel data for Table S2
panel <- bd_panel_model %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    cluster_disp = coerce_cluster(cluster),
    time = dplyr::recode(as.character(wave), !!!time_map),
    time = factor(time, levels = time_levels),
    sex = as.character(sex)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(time), cluster_disp %in% c("Lower-Risk","Higher-Risk"))

#S2.2 Prepare table S2
#S2.2.1 Base dataset for demographics (any_bsd outcome)
demo_base <- panel %>% dplyr::filter(outcome == "any_bsd")

#S2.2.2 Establish N person-periods, N participants, Age
demo_counts <- demo_base %>%
  dplyr::group_by(time, cluster_disp) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_pp = dplyr::n(),
    n_part = dplyr::n_distinct(participant_id),
    age_mean_sd = fmt_mean_sd(age_wave),
    .groups = "drop")

#S2.2.3 Compute age stats
age_row <- demo_counts %>%
  dplyr::transmute(time, cluster_disp, Row = "Age in years, Mean (SD)", val = age_mean_sd)

#S2.2.4 Compute overall N rows and sex information
n_rows <- demo_counts %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(c(n_pp, n_part), names_to = "what", values_to = "val_raw") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Row = dplyr::case_when(
      what == "n_part" ~ "N (participants)",
      what == "n_pp" ~ "N (person-periods)"),
    val = fmt_n(val_raw)) %>%
  dplyr::select(time, cluster_disp, Row, val)
sex_tab <- demo_base %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sex2 = dplyr::case_when(
    !is.na(sex) & sex %in% c("Male","Female") ~ sex,
    TRUE ~ "Other/Unknown")) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(time, cluster_disp, participant_id, sex2) %>%
  dplyr::count(time, cluster_disp, sex2, name = "n_sex") %>%
  dplyr::left_join(demo_counts %>% dplyr::select(time, cluster_disp, n_part), by = c("time","cluster_disp")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(val = fmt_n_pct(n_sex, n_part)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(sex2 %in% c("Male","Female")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Row = paste0("Sex at birth - ", sex2)) %>%
  dplyr::select(time, cluster_disp, Row, val)

#S2.2.5 Combine demographic rows
demo_rows_cs <- dplyr::bind_rows(n_rows, age_row, sex_tab) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Section = "Demographics")

#S2.2.6 Prepare total (across clusters) demographic rows
demo_counts_tot <- demo_base %>%
  dplyr::group_by(time) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_pp = dplyr::n(),
    n_part = dplyr::n_distinct(participant_id),
    age_mean_sd = fmt_mean_sd(age_wave),
    .groups = "drop")

#S2.2.7 Compute total N rows across whole sample
n_rows_total <- demo_counts_tot %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(c(n_pp, n_part), names_to = "what", values_to = "val_raw") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Row = dplyr::case_when(
      what == "n_part" ~ "N (participants)",
      what == "n_pp" ~ "N (person-periods)"),
    val = fmt_n(val_raw),
    cluster_disp = "Total") %>%
  dplyr::select(time, cluster_disp, Row, val)

#S2.2.8 Compute total age row across whole sample
age_row_total <- demo_counts_tot %>%
  dplyr::transmute(time, cluster_disp = "Total", Row = "Age in years, Mean (SD)", val = age_mean_sd)

#S2.2.9 Compute total sex information across whole sample
sex_tab_total <- demo_base %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sex2 = dplyr::case_when(
    !is.na(sex) & sex %in% c("Male","Female") ~ sex,
    TRUE ~ "Other/Unknown")) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(time, participant_id, sex2) %>%
  dplyr::count(time, sex2, name = "n_sex") %>%
  dplyr::left_join(demo_counts_tot %>% dplyr::select(time, n_part), by = "time") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(val = fmt_n_pct(n_sex, n_part)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(sex2 %in% c("Male","Female")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(cluster_disp = "Total", Row = paste0("Sex at birth - ", sex2)) %>%
  dplyr::select(time, cluster_disp, Row, val)

#S2.2.10 Combine total demographic rows across whole sample
demo_rows_total <- dplyr::bind_rows(n_rows_total, age_row_total, sex_tab_total) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Section = "Demographics")

#S2.2.11 Prepare outcome occurrence rows
#S2.2.11.1 Outcome labels
occ_labels <- c(
  "bipolar_I" = "Occurrence - BD I, n (%)",
  "bipolar_II" = "Occurrence - BD II, n (%)",
  "bd_nos" = "Occurrence - BD NOS, n (%)",
  "any_bsd" = "Occurrence - Any BSD, n (%)")

#S2.2.11.2 Occurrence rows by cluster
occ_cs <- panel %>%
  dplyr::filter(outcome %in% names(occ_labels)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(outcome, time, cluster_disp) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_obs = sum(!is.na(.data[[status_var]])),
    n_cases = sum(.data[[status_var]] == 1L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Row = dplyr::recode(outcome, !!!occ_labels),
    val = fmt_n_pct(n_cases, n_obs),
    Section = "Occurrence (wave prevalence)") %>%
  dplyr::select(time, cluster_disp, Row, val, Section)

#S2.2.11.3 Occurrence rows total across whole sample
occ_total <- panel %>%
  dplyr::filter(outcome %in% names(occ_labels)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(outcome, time) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_obs = sum(!is.na(.data[[status_var]])),
    n_cases = sum(.data[[status_var]] == 1L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Row = dplyr::recode(outcome, !!!occ_labels),
    val = fmt_n_pct(n_cases, n_obs),
    cluster_disp = "Total",
    Section = "Occurrence (wave prevalence)") %>%
  dplyr::select(time, cluster_disp, Row, val, Section)

#S2.2.12 Combine all rows into long format
long_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  demo_rows_cs, demo_rows_total,
  occ_cs, occ_total) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    cluster_disp = factor(cluster_disp, levels = c("Total","Lower-Risk","Higher-Risk")),
    time = factor(time, levels = time_levels))

#S2.2.13 Create full grid of rows x sections x cluster_disp x time, fill missing with em-dash
grid <- tidyr::crossing(
  Row = unique(long_all$Row),
  Section = unique(long_all$Section),
  cluster_disp = levels(long_all$cluster_disp),
  time = levels(long_all$time))

#S2.3 Build wide table for GT
#S2.3.1 Left-join long_all to grid and fill missing data with em-dash
long_all <- grid %>%
  dplyr::left_join(long_all, by = c("Row","Section","cluster_disp","time")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(val = dplyr::coalesce(val, "\u2014"))

#S2.3.2 Pivot to wide format with col_keys as cluster_disp::time
wide <- long_all %>%
  dplyr::mutate(col_key = paste0(cluster_disp, "::", time)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Section, Row, col_key, val) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = col_key, values_from = val)

#S2.3.3 Select columns in desired order (if present)
#S2.3.3.1 Desired column keys
col_keys <- c(
  paste0("Total::", time_levels),
  paste0("Lower-Risk::", time_levels),
  paste0("Higher-Risk::", time_levels))

#S2.3.3.2 Keep only columns that exist in wide
have <- intersect(col_keys, names(wide))

#S2.3.3.3 Select only desired columns
wide <- wide %>% dplyr::select(Section, Row, dplyr::all_of(have))

#S2.4 Create GT table
#S2.4.1 Build GT table with spanners and labels
tS2 <- gt::gt(wide, groupname_col = "Section", rowname_col = "Row") |>
  gt::cols_label(.list = setNames(rep(time_levels, 3), have)) |>
  gt::tab_spanner(label = "Total", columns = dplyr::all_of(paste0("Total::", time_levels))) |>
  gt::tab_spanner(label = "Lower-Risk", columns = dplyr::all_of(paste0("Lower-Risk::", time_levels))) |>
  gt::tab_spanner(label = "Higher-Risk",columns = dplyr::all_of(paste0("Higher-Risk::", time_levels))) |>
  gt::cols_align(align = "center", columns = dplyr::all_of(have)) |>
  gt::tab_caption("**Table S2. Characteristics of Participants in the Prevalence of Bipolar Disorders Over Time by Risk Cluster (GEE Analysis)**
Counts are shown per assessment wave (2y, 4y, 6y). Demographics are derived from the *Any BSD* panel at each wave (one row per participant per wave). Occurrence rows report wave prevalence (status==1) within outcome-specific panels, shown as *n (percent)* of observed person-periods (denominator excludes missing).") |>
  gt::tab_options(table.font.size = gt::px(12)) |>
  gt::fmt_missing(columns = dplyr::everything(), missing_text = "\u2014")

#S2.4.2 Save table S2 (with fallback if save_gt not available)
if (exists("save_gt")) {
  save_gt(tS2, "Table_S2_Prevalence_Characteristics_by_Cluster")
} else {
  out_file <- file.path(getwd(), "Table_S2_Prevalence_Characteristics_by_Cluster.html")
  gt::gtsave(tS2, out_file)
  message("Saved: ", out_file)
}

#S2.4.3 Print GT table for Table S2
tS2

```

## Bipolar Disorder Odds by Risk Cluster Over Time stats

- Purpose: Produce per-wave Higher vs Lower cluster odds ratios (with 95% CI, p) from saved GEE interaction models
- Inputs: *_GEE_EXC_INT.rds models for outcomes (BD I/II/NOS/Any BSD); bd_panel_model used only for wave median age labels
- Outputs: Prints inline lines for manuscript
- Notes: Uses emmeans contrasts on the link scale and robust vcov from geeglm; truncates long CIs with arrows to share a common x-axis

```{r bd odds over time stats}

## Bipolar Disorder Odds by Risk Cluster Over Time ##

#OR.1 Setup
#OR.1.1 Directory for saved GEE fits
fit_dir <- "/home/exacloud/gscratch/NagelLab/staff/sam/projects/ABCD_MDS_Risk/results/main_analysis/2_bd_mixed_logit/rds"

#OR.1.2 Directory for each outcome's GEE EXC interaction fit
fit_paths <- c(
  bipolar_I = file.path(fit_dir, "bipolar_I_GEE_EXC_INT.rds"),
  bipolar_II = file.path(fit_dir, "bipolar_II_GEE_EXC_INT.rds"),
  bd_nos = file.path(fit_dir, "bd_nos_GEE_EXC_INT.rds"),
  any_bsd = file.path(fit_dir, "any_bsd_GEE_EXC_INT.rds"))

#OR.1.3 Create a modified safe read function for this section
safe_read_rds_gee <- function(p) tryCatch(readRDS(p), error = function(e) NULL)

#OR.1.4 Establish median age labels per wave
age_meds <- try({
  bd_panel_model %>%
    dplyr::group_by(wave) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(age_med = median(age_wave, na.rm = TRUE), .groups="drop") %>%
    dplyr::transmute(wave = as.character(wave), lab = sprintf("%s (median age %.1f)",
      dplyr::recode(wave, !!!wave_pretty), age_med))
}, silent = TRUE)

#OR.1.5 Fallback if age_meds computation fails
age_meds <- if (inherits(age_meds, "try-error")) tibble::tibble(wave = names(wave_pretty), lab = wave_pretty[names(wave_pretty)]) else age_meds

#OR.2 Helper: compute emmeans odds ratios (Higher- vs Lower-Risk) by wave from each GEE interaction model
em_or_by_wave <- function(fit) {

  #OR.2.1 Use robust vcov from the model (vcov.geeglm is robust by default) to compute emmeans
  V <- try(vcov(fit), silent = TRUE)
  emm <- emmeans::emmeans(fit, ~ cluster | wave, type = "link", vcov. = if (!inherits(V, "try-error")) V else NULL)
  
  #OR.2.2 Force contrast to be C2 - C1 (so OR = odds_C2 / odds_C1) regardless of reference
  lev <- levels(emm@grid$cluster)
  if (!all(c("C1","C2") %in% lev)) stop("Cluster levels not found in fit: ", paste(lev, collapse=", "))
  
  #OR.2.3 Compute contrast and summary
  w <- setNames(numeric(length(lev)), lev); w["C2"] <- 1; w["C1"] <- -1
  cmp <- emmeans::contrast(emm, method = list("C2 - C1" = w))
  sm  <- summary(cmp, infer = TRUE)  # on link (logit) scale
  
  #OR.2.4 Return tidy tibble with OR, CI, p per wave
  tibble::tibble(
    wave = as.character(sm$wave),
    OR = exp(sm$estimate),
    CI_low = exp(sm$lower.CL),
    CI_high= exp(sm$upper.CL),
    p = sm$p.value)
}

#OR.3 Load each GEE fit, compute emmeans OR contrasts, and combine into a single data frame
out_labs <- OUT_LABS
gee_pairs <- purrr::imap_dfr(fit_paths, function(fp, oc) {
  fit <- safe_read_rds_gee(fp)
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  em_or_by_wave(fit) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      outcome = oc,
      out_lab = out_labs[oc],
      wave_pretty = dplyr::recode(wave, !!!wave_pretty)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(age_meds, by = c("wave" = "wave")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(wave_lab = ifelse(!is.na(lab), lab, wave_pretty))
})

#OR.3.1 Check if any results were computed, stop and provide a message why if not
if (!nrow(gee_pairs)) stop("Could not load any *_GEE_EXC_INT.rds fits or compute emmeans contrasts.")

#OR.4 Generate in-line stats for manuscript reporting
fmt_p <- function(p) ifelse(is.na(p), "-", ifelse(p < .001, "<.001", sprintf("%.3f", p)))
fmt_ci <- function(lo, hi) sprintf("%s-%s", sprintf("%.2f", lo), sprintf("%.2f", hi))
inline_lines <- gee_pairs %>%
  dplyr::mutate(line = glue("{out_lab} {wave_pretty}: OR={sprintf('%.2f', OR)}, 95% CI {fmt_ci(CI_low, CI_high)}, p={fmt_p(p)}")) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(factor(out_lab, levels = out_labs), factor(wave, levels = names(wave_pretty))) %>%
  dplyr::pull(line)

#OR.4.1 Print inline lines
cat("GEE in-line stats (Higher- vs Lower-Risk, emmeans, per wave):\n",
    paste0(" • ", inline_lines, collapse = "\n"), "\n\n")

```

## Missing-Data Imputation Sensitivity Inputs

- Purpose: Use the missForest missing-data sensitivity-analysis outputs that were read in during `#2. Inputs Configuration`
- Inputs: `miss_var_imp`, `imp_inclusion`, `little_mcar_imp`, and `kgrid_mf`
- Outputs: In-memory objects passed directly to Table S3, Table S4, and Figure S2
- Notes: All file discovery and reading occur upstream in the centralized inputs section so downstream supplement code remains display-focused

## Table S3 - Baseline Missingness Across the 17 Risk Variables Used for Clustering

- Purpose: Reproduce the current supplementary missingness table using the exact missForest sensitivity-analysis export
- Inputs: `miss_var_imp`
- Outputs: Saves `Table_S3_Baseline_Missingness_Clustering_Variables` as HTML/PNG/RTF in results/tables; returns a gt table
- Notes: The saved pct_miss field is a proportion, so it is displayed here as a percentage for publication-quality formatting

```{r table s3}

## Table S3: Baseline missingness across the 17 risk variables used for clustering ##

#S3.1 Prepare the missingness table using exact saved outputs
tS3_df <- miss_var_imp %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    `Baseline Risk Variable` = dplyr::recode(feature, !!!risk_label_map_imp),
    `N Missing` = as.integer(n_miss),
    `Percent Missing` = as.numeric(pct_miss)) %>%
 dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(`Percent Missing`), `Baseline Risk Variable`)

#S3.2 Create table caption
tS3_caption <- paste0(
  "**Table S3. Baseline missingness across the 17 risk variables used for clustering**  \n",
  "Missingness is summarized across all baseline participants screened for the imputation sensitivity analysis. ",
  "Percent missing is displayed as a percentage of the baseline screening denominator.")

#S3.3 Build gt table
tS3_gt <- gt::gt(tS3_df) |>
  gt::fmt_number(columns = `N Missing`, decimals = 0, use_seps = TRUE) |>
  gt::fmt_percent(columns = `Percent Missing`, decimals = 2) |>
  gt::cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(`N Missing`, `Percent Missing`)) |>
  gt::tab_caption(tS3_caption) |>
  gt::tab_options(table.font.size = gt::px(12))

#S3.4 Save and print Table S3
save_gt(tS3_gt, "Table_S3_Baseline_Missingness_Clustering_Variables")
tS3_gt

```

## Table S4 - k-Prototypes Fit, Stability, and Anchor-Concordance Metrics for MissForest-Imputed Baseline Data Across Candidate k Solutions

- Purpose: Reproduce the current supplementary missForest k-grid summary table using the exact saved output from the imputation script
- Inputs: `kgrid_mf`
- Outputs: Saves `Table_S4_MissForest_KGrid_Metrics` as HTML/PNG/RTF in results/tables; returns a gt table
- Notes: This table uses only the missForest solution, consistent with the current supplement and revised analysis plan

```{r table s4}

## Table S4: k-prototypes fit, stability, and anchor-concordance metrics for missForest-imputed baseline data across candidate k solutions ##

#S4.1 Prepare the exact missForest k-grid summary table
tS4_df <- kgrid_mf %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    `k Value` = as.integer(k),
    `Smallest Cluster n` = as.integer(min_cluster_n),
    `lambda` = as.numeric(lambda),
    `Mean Silhouette` = as.numeric(silhouette_huang),
    `Mean Jaccard` = as.numeric(jacc_mean),
    `ARI vs Anchor` = as.numeric(ari_vs_anchor_k2)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(`k Value`)

#S4.2 Create caption
tS4_caption <- paste0(
  "**Table S4. k-prototypes fit, stability, and anchor-concordance metrics for missForest-imputed baseline data across candidate k solutions**  \n",
  "Candidate k-prototypes solutions were refit after missForest imputation of eligible baseline participants. ",
  "Mean Jaccard reflects bootstrap stability, and ARI reflects agreement with the original complete-case k=2 anchor partition.")

#S4.3 Build gt table
tS4_gt <- gt::gt(tS4_df) |>
  gt::fmt_number(columns = `k Value`, decimals = 0, use_seps = FALSE) |>
  gt::fmt_number(columns = `Smallest Cluster n`, decimals = 0, use_seps = TRUE) |>
  gt::fmt_number(
    columns = c(`lambda`, `Mean Silhouette`, `Mean Jaccard`, `ARI vs Anchor`),
    decimals = 3) |>
  gt::cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(`k Value`, `Smallest Cluster n`, `lambda`, `Mean Silhouette`, `Mean Jaccard`, `ARI vs Anchor`)) |>
  gt::tab_caption(tS4_caption) |>
  gt::tab_options(table.font.size = gt::px(12))

#S4.4 Save and print Table S4
save_gt(tS4_gt, "Table_S4_MissForest_KGrid_Metrics")
tS4_gt

```

## Figure S1 - MissForest-Imputed Clustering Sensitivity Metrics Across Candidate k Solutions

- Purpose: Visualize the saved missForest clustering-summary metrics across candidate k values
- Inputs: `kgrid_mf`
- Outputs: Saves `Figure_S1_MissForest_KGrid_Metrics.{pdf,png}` in results/figures; returns a ggplot object
- Notes: This is the figure supported by the saved outputs. The current imputation script does not save replicate-level silhouette or ARI distributions, so this figure summarizes the exported metric values across k rather than plotting distributions

```{r figure s1}

## Figure S1: MissForest-imputed clustering sensitivity metrics across candidate k solutions ##

#FS1.1 Reshape saved k-grid metrics into long plotting format
figS1_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  kgrid_mf %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      k = as.integer(k),
      metric = "Mean silhouette",
      value = as.numeric(silhouette_huang),
      ymin = NA_real_,
      ymax = NA_real_),
  kgrid_mf %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      k = as.integer(k),
      metric = "Mean Jaccard",
      value = as.numeric(jacc_mean),
      ymin = pmax(as.numeric(jacc_mean) - as.numeric(jacc_sd), 0),
      ymax = pmin(as.numeric(jacc_mean) + as.numeric(jacc_sd), 1)),
  kgrid_mf %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      k = as.integer(k),
      metric = "ARI vs anchor",
      value = as.numeric(ari_vs_anchor_k2),
      ymin = NA_real_,
      ymax = NA_real_)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    metric = factor(metric, levels = c("Mean silhouette", "Mean Jaccard", "ARI vs anchor")))

#FS1.2 Build a clean multi-panel summary figure
figS1 <- ggplot(figS1_df, aes(x = k, y = value)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(
    shape = 21,
    size = 3.0,
    stroke = 0.7,
    fill = "white") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(figS1_df$k))) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.015, 0.03))) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ metric,
    ncol = 3,
    scales = "fixed",
    labeller = as_labeller(c(
      "Mean silhouette" = "A. Mean silhouette",
      "Mean Jaccard" = "B. Mean Jaccard",
      "ARI vs anchor" = "C. ARI vs anchor"))) +
  labs(
    title = "Figure S1. MissForest-imputed clustering sensitivity metrics across candidate k solutions",
    x = "Candidate number of clusters (k)",
    y = NULL) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, margin = margin(t = 2, b = 2)),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.spacing = grid::unit(0.55, "lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(4, 8, 4, 8))

#FS1.3 Save and print Figure S1
save_fig(figS1, "Figure_S1_MissForest_KGrid_Metrics", width = 11.5, height = 7)
figS1

```

## Table S5 - Feature importance summary across MCR, centroid separability, and XGBoost + SHAP analyses and resulting minimally viable feature subset (MVFS) composition

- Purpose: Summarize the integrated feature-priority results used for parsimonious feature selection and identify which variables were retained in the final MVFS
- Inputs: `mvfs_priority_tbl`, `mvfs_selected_solutions`, and `risk_label_map_imp`
- Outputs: Saves `Table_S5_Feature_Importance_and_MVFS_Composition` as HTML/PNG/RTF in `results/tables`; returns a gt table
- Notes: The final MVFS composition is taken directly from the saved selected-solution RDS when available; if that object is unavailable, the code falls back to the first `mvfs_n` features by final priority rank

```{r table s5}

## Table S5: Feature importance summary and resulting MVFS composition ##

#S5.1 Determine the final retained MVFS feature set from the saved selected solution
#S5.1.1 Prefer the actual saved selected-solution object
mvfs_features_final <- character(0)

if (is.list(mvfs_selected_solutions) && length(mvfs_selected_solutions) >= 1) {
  sol1 <- mvfs_selected_solutions[[1]]

  if (is.list(sol1) && "features" %in% names(sol1)) {
    mvfs_features_final <- sol1$features %>% as.character()
  }
}

#S5.1.2 Fallback: if the selected-solution object is unavailable, use the selected MVFS size and the ranked priority table
if (!length(mvfs_features_final)) {
  mvfs_n_fallback <- mvfs_selected_summary %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(mvfs_n)) %>%
    dplyr::slice(1) %>%
    dplyr::pull(mvfs_n)

  if (length(mvfs_n_fallback) == 1 && is.finite(mvfs_n_fallback)) {
    mvfs_features_final <- mvfs_priority_tbl %>%
      dplyr::arrange(rank_final_priority) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = as.integer(mvfs_n_fallback)) %>%
      dplyr::pull(feature) %>%
      as.character()
  }
}

#S5.2 Prepare publication-ready Table S5 data
tS5_df <- mvfs_priority_tbl %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    `Variable` = dplyr::recode(feature, !!!risk_label_map_imp),
    `MCR Mean` = as.numeric(mcr_mean),
    `|Δz|` = as.numeric(delta_z),
    `Max Cohen's d` = as.numeric(max_cohens_d),
    `Mean |SHAP|` = as.numeric(mean_abs_shap),
    `Ranked Importance` = as.integer(rank_final_priority),
    `Included in Final MVFS?` = dplyr::if_else(
      feature %in% mvfs_features_final,
      "Yes",
      "No",
      missing = "No")) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(`Ranked Importance`)

#S5.3 Create caption
tS5_caption <- paste0(
  "**Table S5. Feature importance summary across misclassification rate (MCR), centroid separability, and XGBoost + SHAP analyses and resulting minimally viable feature subset (MVFS) composition**  \n",
  "Final MVFS membership is taken from the saved selected parsimonious solution when available. ",
  "For binary features, centroid-separation statistics based on standardized mean separation are not applicable and are displayed as em dashes.")

#S5.4 Build gt table
tS5_gt <- gt::gt(tS5_df) |>
  gt::fmt_number(
    columns = c(`MCR Mean`, `|Δz|`, `Max Cohen's d`, `Mean |SHAP|`),
    decimals = 3) |>
  gt::fmt_number(
    columns = `Ranked Importance`,
    decimals = 0,
    use_seps = FALSE) |>
  gt::sub_missing(
    columns = c(`|Δz|`, `Max Cohen's d`),
    missing_text = "\u2014") |>
  gt::cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(`MCR Mean`, `|Δz|`, `Max Cohen's d`, `Mean |SHAP|`, `Ranked Importance`, `Included in Final MVFS?`)) |>
  gt::tab_caption(tS5_caption) |>
  gt::tab_options(table.font.size = gt::px(12))

#S5.5 Save and print Table S5
save_gt(tS5_gt, "Table_S5_Feature_Importance_and_MVFS_Composition")
tS5_gt

```

## Figure S2 - Feature-importance and parsimonious-feature-set sensitivity displays

- Purpose: Display the seed-level MCR distributions, the saved SHAP beeswarm plot, and the 2-D embedding of the selected MVFS solution with cluster centroids
- Inputs: `fi_seed_level`, `mvfs_priority_tbl`, `shap_beeswarm_png`, and `mvfs7_umap_k2`
- Outputs: Saves `Figure_S2_Feature_Importance_and_MVFS_Sensitivity.{pdf,png}` in `results/figures`; returns a combined figure object
- Notes: The SHAP beeswarm panel is embedded from the saved analysis figure because the feature-importance pipeline saved the visualization but not an exported observation-level SHAP matrix suitable for exact downstream reconstruction

```{r figure s2}

## Figure S2: Feature-importance and parsimonious-feature-set sensitivity displays ##

#FS2.1 Create a stable feature ordering shared across all three panels
#FS2.1.1 Order features by final integrated priority rank
feature_order_tbl <- mvfs_priority_tbl %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    feature,
    feature_label = dplyr::recode(feature, !!!risk_label_map_imp),
    rank_final_priority = as.integer(rank_final_priority),
    mcr_mean = as.numeric(mcr_mean),
    mean_abs_shap = as.numeric(mean_abs_shap),
    sep_abs = as.numeric(sep_abs)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(rank_final_priority)

feature_levels_plot <- rev(feature_order_tbl$feature_label)
feature_names_plot <- feature_order_tbl$feature
pretty_feature_order <- dplyr::recode(
  feature_names_plot,
  !!!risk_label_map_imp,
  .default = feature_names_plot)

#FS2.2 Create a shared clean theme for all panels
panel_theme_s2 <- function(base_size = 16) {
  theme_classic(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size - 1),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = base_size - 1),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = base_size - 1),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = base_size - 1.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = base_size - 2),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = base_size - 2),
      legend.title = element_text(size = base_size - 1),
      legend.text = element_text(size = base_size - 2),
      plot.margin = margin(6, 8, 6, 8))
}

#FS2.3 Panel A: seed-level MCR distributions across features
#FS2.3.1 Prepare plotting data
mcr_seed_plot_df <- fi_seed_level %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(feature), !is.na(mcr_mean)) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    feature,
    feature_label = dplyr::recode(feature, !!!risk_label_map_imp),
    mcr_mean = as.numeric(mcr_mean),
    seed_idx = as.integer(seed_idx)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    feature_label = factor(feature_label, levels = feature_levels_plot))

#FS2.3.2 Compute per-feature mean MCR for overlay
mcr_seed_means <- mcr_seed_plot_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(feature_label) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mcr_mean_overall = mean(mcr_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")

#FS2.3.3 Build Panel A
p_mcr <- ggplot(mcr_seed_plot_df, aes(x = mcr_mean, y = feature_label)) +
  geom_violin(
    fill = "#D9D9D9",
    alpha = 0.75,
    color = NA,
    trim = TRUE,
    scale = "width") +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.2,
    outlier.shape = NA,
    fill = "white",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(
    data = mcr_seed_means,
    aes(x = mcr_mean_overall, y = feature_label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.7,
    stroke = 0.6,
    fill = "black",
    color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "A. Permutation MCR Distributions",
    x = "Misclassification rate (MCR)",
    y = NULL) +
  panel_theme_s2()

#FS2.4 Panel B: ranked centroid separability display
#FS2.4.1 Prepare separability plotting data
sep_plot_df <- feature_order_tbl %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    feature_label = factor(feature_label, levels = feature_levels_plot),
    sep_abs = as.numeric(sep_abs),
    sep_missing = is.na(sep_abs))

#FS2.4.2 Build Panel B
p_sep <- ggplot(sep_plot_df, aes(x = sep_abs, y = feature_label)) +
  geom_segment(
    data = sep_plot_df %>% dplyr::filter(!sep_missing),
    aes(x = 0, xend = sep_abs, y = feature_label, yend = feature_label),
    linewidth = 0.5,
    color = "grey55") +
  geom_point(
    data = sep_plot_df %>% dplyr::filter(!sep_missing),
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.7,
    stroke = 0.6,
    fill = "white",
    color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "B. Centroid Separability",
    x = "|Δz|",
    y = NULL) +
  panel_theme_s2() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

#FS2.5 Panel C: exact SHAP beeswarm from the saved feature-importance analysis
if (inherits(fi_shapviz, "shapviz")) {

  sv_exact <- fi_shapviz

  #FS2.5.1 Apply pretty labels to the saved SHAP object
  old_names <- colnames(sv_exact$S)
  new_names <- dplyr::recode(
    old_names,
    !!!risk_label_map_imp,
    .default = old_names)

  colnames(sv_exact$S) <- new_names

  if (!is.null(sv_exact$X)) {
    if (is.data.frame(sv_exact$X)) {
      names(sv_exact$X) <- dplyr::recode(
        names(sv_exact$X),
        !!!risk_label_map_imp,
        .default = names(sv_exact$X))
    } else if (is.matrix(sv_exact$X)) {
      colnames(sv_exact$X) <- dplyr::recode(
        colnames(sv_exact$X),
        !!!risk_label_map_imp,
        .default = colnames(sv_exact$X))
    }
  }

  #FS2.5.2 Preserve the final hierarchical feature order used in Panels A and B
  keep_cols <- pretty_feature_order[pretty_feature_order %in% colnames(sv_exact$S)]
  sv_exact$S <- sv_exact$S[, keep_cols, drop = FALSE]

  if (!is.null(sv_exact$X)) {
    if (is.data.frame(sv_exact$X)) {
      keep_x <- keep_cols[keep_cols %in% names(sv_exact$X)]
      sv_exact$X <- sv_exact$X[, keep_x, drop = FALSE]
    } else if (is.matrix(sv_exact$X)) {
      keep_x <- keep_cols[keep_cols %in% colnames(sv_exact$X)]
      sv_exact$X <- sv_exact$X[, keep_x, drop = FALSE]
    }
  }

  #FS2.5.3 Build the exact SHAP beeswarm
  p_shap <- shapviz::sv_importance(
    sv_exact,
    kind = "beeswarm",
    max_display = ncol(sv_exact$S),
    show_numbers = FALSE,
    sort_features = FALSE) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_discrete(limits = feature_levels_plot) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno") +
    labs(
      title = "C. SHAP Importance Distributions",
      x = "SHAP value",
      y = NULL,
      color = "Relative feature\nvalue") +
    panel_theme_s2() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
      legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA))

} else {
  stop(
    "Exact Figure S2 SHAP reproduction requires ",
    "results/main_analysis/1_clustering/4_feature_importance/shap/xgb_surrogate_shapviz.rds. ",
    "Finish re-running 4_feature_importance.rmd, then knit again.")
}

#FS2.6 Combine panels into the final figure
figS2 <- p_mcr + p_sep + p_shap +
  patchwork::plot_layout(ncol = 3, widths = c(1.15, 0.85, 1.55)) +
  patchwork::plot_annotation(
    title = "Figure S2. Feature-importance and parsimonious-feature-set sensitivity displays",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15)))

#FS2.7 Save and print Figure S2
save_fig(figS2, "Figure_S2_Feature_Importance_and_MVFS_Sensitivity", width = 18, height = 10, dpi = 720)
figS2

```

## Figure S3 - MVFS agreement and stability trace across candidate subset size

- Purpose: Show how agreement with the anchor partition and minimum clusterwise stability vary across candidate MVFS subset size for the selected primary search trace
- Inputs: `mvfs_trace_all` and `mvfs_selected_summary`
- Outputs: Saves `Figure_S3_MVFS_Trace_ARI_and_Jaccard.{pdf,png}` in `results/figures`; returns a combined figure object
- Notes: This figure intentionally focuses on the selected primary trace rather than retaining facet labels from the intermediate analysis plots, which is cleaner and more aligned with publication display standards

```{r figure s3}

## Figure S3: MVFS agreement and stability trace across candidate subset size ##

#FS3.1 Identify the selected primary MVFS trace
primary_mvfs_trace <- mvfs_selected_summary %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(mvfs_n))

#FS3.1.1 Guard against multiple selected traces to avoid silently plotting the wrong one
if (nrow(primary_mvfs_trace) != 1) {
  stop(
    "Figure S3 expects exactly one selected MVFS trace in mvfs_selected_summary; found ",
    nrow(primary_mvfs_trace), ".")
}

#FS3.1.2 Extract the selected trace metadata
primary_search <- primary_mvfs_trace$search[[1]]
primary_lambda <- primary_mvfs_trace$lambda_mode[[1]]
primary_mvfs_n <- as.integer(primary_mvfs_trace$mvfs_n[[1]])

#FS3.2 Restrict the full trace table to the selected primary trace
mvfs_trace_plot_df <- mvfs_trace_all %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    search == primary_search,
    lambda_mode == primary_lambda) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(n_features)

mvfs_trace_point <- mvfs_trace_plot_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(n_features == primary_mvfs_n)

#FS3.3 Build Panel A: ARI trace
p_mvfs_ari <- ggplot(mvfs_trace_plot_df, aes(x = n_features, y = ari)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 1.9) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.80, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = primary_mvfs_n, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(
    data = mvfs_trace_point,
    size = 3,
    shape = 21,
    stroke = 0.8,
    fill = "white") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.04))) +
  labs(
    title = "A. Agreement with anchor partition",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Adjusted Rand index") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

#FS3.4 Build Panel B: minimum Jaccard trace
p_mvfs_jacc <- ggplot(mvfs_trace_plot_df, aes(x = n_features, y = jaccard_min)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 1.9) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.80, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = primary_mvfs_n, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(
    data = mvfs_trace_point,
    size = 3,
    shape = 21,
    stroke = 0.8,
    fill = "white") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.04))) +
  labs(
    title = "B. Minimum clusterwise stability",
    x = "Candidate subset size, |S| (number of features)",
    y = "Minimum Jaccard similarity") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

#FS3.5 Combine into final Figure S3
figS3 <- p_mvfs_ari / p_mvfs_jacc +
  patchwork::plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1)) +
  patchwork::plot_annotation(
    title = "Figure S3. Minimally viable feature subset (MVFS) agreement and stability trace across subset size",
    subtitle = glue::glue(
      "Primary trace: search = {primary_search}; lambda mode = {primary_lambda}; selected MVFS = {primary_mvfs_n}; thresholds shown at 0.80"),
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10)))

#FS3.6 Save and print Figure S3
save_fig(figS3, "Figure_S3_MVFS_Trace_ARI_and_Jaccard", width = 9.5, height = 8.5)
figS3

```

## Table S6: Cross-algorithm clustering benchmark metrics across candidate k solutions

- Purpose: Summarize cross-algorithm benchmark results across candidate k solutions and identify the preferred solution within each clustering algorithm under the shared comparison framework
- Inputs: `alg_bench_metrics`, containing algorithm-specific benchmark outputs across candidate k values
- Outputs: Saves `Table_S6_Algorithm_Benchmark_Metrics` as HTML/PNG/RTF in results/tables; returns a gt table
- Notes: Standardizes algorithm labels across k-prototypes, KAMILA, and clustMD; uses mean Gower silhouette as the common primary comparison metric; bolds the preferred row within each algorithm; retains BIC descriptively for clustMD only because it is not available for the other algorithms

```{r table s6}

## Table S6: Cross-algorithm clustering benchmark metrics across candidate k solutions ##

#S6.1 Prepare the benchmark table
#S6.1.1 Clean and standardize algorithm labels
tS6_df0 <- alg_bench_metrics %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    algorithm_key = stringr::str_to_lower(as.character(algorithm)),
    algorithm_disp = dplyr::case_when(
      algorithm_key %in% c("kproto", "k-prototypes", "kprototypes") ~ "k-prototypes",
      algorithm_key %in% c("kamila") ~ "KAMILA",
      algorithm_key %in% c("clustmd") ~ "clustMD",
      TRUE ~ as.character(algorithm)),
    algorithm_disp = factor(
      algorithm_disp,
      levels = c("k-prototypes", "KAMILA", "clustMD")),
    k = as.integer(k),
    bic = as.numeric(bic),
    gower_silhouette = as.numeric(gower_silhouette),
    ari_vs_anchor_k2 = as.numeric(ari_vs_anchor_k2),
    min_cluster_n = as.integer(min_cluster_n),
    max_cluster_n = as.integer(max_cluster_n)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(algorithm_disp) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(gower_silhouette), k, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(preferred_k = dplyr::row_number() == 1L) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::arrange(algorithm_disp, k)

#S6.1.2 Build publication-ready display data
tS6_df <- tS6_df0 %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    Algorithm = algorithm_disp,
    `k Value` = k,
    `BIC` = bic,
    `Mean Gower Silhouette` = gower_silhouette,
    `ARI vs Anchor` = ari_vs_anchor_k2,
    `Minimum Cluster n` = min_cluster_n,
    `Maximum Cluster n` = max_cluster_n,
    preferred_k = preferred_k)

#S6.1.3 Save row indices for preferred solutions so they can be emphasized in the table
preferred_rows_s6 <- which(tS6_df$preferred_k)

#S6.2 Create caption
tS6_caption <- paste0(
  "**Table S6. Cross-algorithm clustering benchmark metrics across candidate k solutions**  \n",
  "Candidate solutions from k-prototypes, KAMILA, and clustMD were compared on the same complete-case, z-scored mixed-data frame. ",
  "The common primary comparison metric was mean silhouette computed on Gower distance. ",
  "Within each algorithm, the preferred solution was defined as the candidate maximizing mean Gower silhouette, with smaller *k* used to break ties.")

#S6.3 Build gt table
tS6_gt <- gt::gt(
  tS6_df %>% dplyr::select(-preferred_k)) |>
  gt::fmt_number(
    columns = `k Value`,
    decimals = 0,
    use_seps = FALSE) |>
  gt::fmt_number(
    columns = `BIC`,
    decimals = 3,
    use_seps = TRUE) |>
  gt::fmt_number(
    columns = c(`Mean Gower Silhouette`, `ARI vs Anchor`),
    decimals = 3) |>
  gt::fmt_number(
    columns = c(`Minimum Cluster n`, `Maximum Cluster n`),
    decimals = 0,
    use_seps = TRUE) |>
  gt::sub_missing(
    columns = `BIC`,
    missing_text = "\u2014") |>
  gt::cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(
      `Algorithm`,
      `k Value`,
      `BIC`,
      `Mean Gower Silhouette`,
      `ARI vs Anchor`,
      `Minimum Cluster n`,
      `Maximum Cluster n`)) |>
  gt::tab_caption(tS6_caption) |>
  gt::tab_source_note(
  gt::md(paste(
    "Rows shown in **bold** indicate the preferred solution within each algorithm under the shared Gower-silhouette framework.",
    "ARI is a descriptive agreement metric relative to the original k-prototypes *k*=2 anchor partition and was not the optimization target.",
    "BIC was available only for clustMD and is shown descriptively.",
    sep = "  \n"))) |>
  gt::tab_options(table.font.size = gt::px(12))

#S6.4 Emphasize preferred rows
tS6_gt <- tS6_gt |>
  gt::tab_style(
    style = gt::cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = gt::cells_body(
      rows = preferred_rows_s6,
      columns = dplyr::everything()))

#S6.5 Save and print Table S6
save_gt(tS6_gt, "Table_S6_Algorithm_Benchmark_Metrics")
tS6_gt

```

## Figure S4: 

- Purpose: Visualize how the shared benchmark metrics vary across candidate k values for each clustering algorithm
- Inputs: `alg_bench_metrics`
- Outputs: Saves `Figure_S4_Algorithm_Benchmark_Metrics.{pdf,png}` in results/figures; returns a combined figure object
- Notes: Panel A displays mean Gower silhouette, the shared primary cross-algorithm comparison metric; Panel B displays ARI versus the original k-prototypes k=2 anchor partition as a descriptive concordance metric; open circles mark the preferred solution within each algorithm under the shared Gower-silhouette framework

```{r figure s4}

## Figure S4: Cross-algorithm benchmark sensitivity metrics across candidate k solutions ##

#FS4.1 Prepare plotting data
alg_bench_plot_df <- alg_bench_metrics %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    algorithm_key = stringr::str_to_lower(as.character(algorithm)),
    algorithm_disp = dplyr::case_when(
      algorithm_key %in% c("kproto", "k-prototypes", "kprototypes") ~ "k-prototypes",
      algorithm_key %in% c("kamila") ~ "KAMILA",
      algorithm_key %in% c("clustmd") ~ "clustMD",
      TRUE ~ as.character(algorithm)),
    algorithm_disp = factor(
      algorithm_disp,
      levels = c("k-prototypes", "KAMILA", "clustMD")),
    k = as.integer(k),
    gower_silhouette = as.numeric(gower_silhouette),
    ari_vs_anchor_k2 = as.numeric(ari_vs_anchor_k2)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(algorithm_disp, k)

#FS4.1.1 Identify preferred solutions under the shared Gower-silhouette rule
alg_bench_pref_df <- alg_bench_plot_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(algorithm_disp) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(gower_silhouette), k, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

#FS4.2 Set colors and theme
alg_cols_s4 <- setNames(
  viridisLite::viridis(3, option = "D"),
  c("k-prototypes", "KAMILA", "clustMD"))

panel_theme_s4 <- function(base_size = 12) {
  theme_classic(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_text(size = base_size - 1),
      legend.text = element_text(size = base_size - 1),
      axis.title = element_text(size = base_size),
      axis.text = element_text(size = base_size - 1),
      plot.margin = margin(6, 8, 6, 8))
}

#FS4.3 Panel A: shared primary benchmark metric
p_alg_sil <- ggplot(
  alg_bench_plot_df,
  aes(x = k, y = gower_silhouette, color = algorithm_disp)) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.4,
    alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 1.9) +
  geom_point(
    data = alg_bench_pref_df,
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.8,
    stroke = 0.8,
    fill = "white") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = sort(unique(alg_bench_plot_df$k))) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = alg_cols_s4,
    breaks = names(alg_cols_s4),
    labels = names(alg_cols_s4)) +
  labs(
    title = "A. Mean Gower silhouette by candidate k",
    x = "Candidate number of clusters (k)",
    y = "Mean Gower silhouette",
    color = "Algorithm") +
  panel_theme_s4()

#FS4.4 Panel B: descriptive agreement with the anchor partition
p_alg_ari <- ggplot(
  alg_bench_plot_df,
  aes(x = k, y = ari_vs_anchor_k2, color = algorithm_disp)) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.4,
    alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 1.9) +
  geom_point(
    data = alg_bench_pref_df,
    aes(x = k, y = ari_vs_anchor_k2, color = algorithm_disp),
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.8,
    stroke = 0.8,
    fill = "white",
    inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = sort(unique(alg_bench_plot_df$k))) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.04))) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = alg_cols_s4,
    breaks = names(alg_cols_s4),
    labels = names(alg_cols_s4)) +
  labs(
    title = "B. ARI vs original k-prototypes k=2 anchor",
    x = "Candidate number of clusters (k)",
    y = "Adjusted Rand index",
    color = "Algorithm") +
  panel_theme_s4()

#FS4.5 Combine into final Figure S4
figS4 <- p_alg_sil + p_alg_ari +
  patchwork::plot_layout(guides = "collect", ncol = 2) +
  patchwork::plot_annotation(
    title = "Figure S4. Cross-algorithm benchmark sensitivity metrics across candidate k solutions",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Panel A shows the shared primary cross-algorithm comparison metric. ",
      "Panel B shows descriptive agreement with the original k-prototypes k=2 anchor partition. ",
      "Open circles indicate the preferred solution within each algorithm under the shared Gower-silhouette framework."),
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10))) &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

#FS4.6 Save and print Figure S4
save_fig(figS4, "Figure_S4_Algorithm_Benchmark_Metrics", width = 12.5, height = 5.4)
figS4

```

## Figure S5: Common-comorbidity sensitivity-analysis odds ratios by wave

- Purpose: Display the wave-specific higher-risk vs lower-risk cluster odds ratios for the common-comorbidity sensitivity-analysis contrasts emphasized in the supplement text
- Inputs: `comorb_or_by_wave`, containing emmeans-derived cluster contrasts by wave from the common-comorbidity GEE models
- Outputs: Saves `Figure_S5_Common_Comorbidity_Sensitivity_ORs.{pdf,png}` in results/figures; returns a combined figure object
- Notes: Restricts display to the 3 contrasts reported in the supplement results; uses a log-scale odds-ratio axis with a reference line at OR=1; Panel A shows contrasts versus no diagnosis, and Panel B shows the direct pairwise comparison between BSD plus comorbidity and other diagnosis only

```{r figure s5}

## Figure S5: Common-comorbidity sensitivity-analysis odds ratios by wave ##

#FS5.1 Guard against placeholder input
if (is_tk(comorb_or_by_wave)) {
  stop(
    "Figure S5 requires results/main_analysis/2_statistical_analysis/5_comorbidity_sensitivity_analysis/tables/gee_or_by_wave.csv. ",
    "Run 5_comorbidity_sensitivity_analysis.Rmd first, then knit again.")
}

#FS5.2 Prepare plotting data
#FS5.2.1 Keep only the contrasts reported in the supplementary results
figS5_df <- comorb_or_by_wave %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  dplyr::filter(outcome %in% c(
    "other_only_vs_nodx",
    "bsd_plus_other_vs_nodx",
    "bsd_plus_vs_other_only")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    outcome_label = dplyr::recode(
      outcome,
      "other_only_vs_nodx" = "Other diagnosis only vs no diagnosis",
      "bsd_plus_other_vs_nodx" = "BSD plus any other diagnosis vs no diagnosis",
      "bsd_plus_vs_other_only" = "BSD plus any other diagnosis vs other diagnosis only"),
    panel_label = dplyr::case_when(
      outcome %in% c("other_only_vs_nodx", "bsd_plus_other_vs_nodx") ~ "A. Contrasts versus no diagnosis",
      outcome == "bsd_plus_vs_other_only" ~ "B. Direct pairwise comparison"),
    wave_label = dplyr::recode(
      as.character(wave),
      "ses-02A" = "Year-2",
      "ses-04A" = "Year-4",
      "ses-06A" = "Year-6"),
    wave_label = factor(wave_label, levels = c("Year-6", "Year-4", "Year-2")),
    outcome_label = factor(
      outcome_label,
      levels = c(
        "Other diagnosis only vs no diagnosis",
        "BSD plus any other diagnosis vs no diagnosis",
        "BSD plus any other diagnosis vs other diagnosis only")),
    panel_label = factor(
      panel_label,
      levels = c(
        "A. Contrasts versus no diagnosis",
        "B. Direct pairwise comparison")),
    or = as.numeric(or),
    or_lcl = as.numeric(or_lcl),
    or_ucl = as.numeric(or_ucl),
    p = as.numeric(p))

#FS5.2.2 Save a clean plotting/stats file for auditing if desired
readr::write_csv(
  figS5_df,
  file.path(tab_dir, "Figure_S5_Common_Comorbidity_Sensitivity_ORs_SourceData.csv"))

#FS5.2.3 Define a shared linear x-axis range across both panels
fs5_xmax <- max(figS5_df$or_ucl, na.rm = TRUE)
fs5_xmax <- ceiling(fs5_xmax / 5) * 5
fs5_x_breaks <- seq(0, fs5_xmax, by = 2.5)

#FS5.3 Shared theme helper
forest_theme_s5 <- function(base_size = 11, strip_size = 9.5) {
  ggplot2::theme_classic(base_size = base_size) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      panel.grid = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      axis.title.y = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = base_size - 1),
      axis.title.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = base_size),
      axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = base_size - 1),
      strip.background = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      strip.text.y.left = ggplot2::element_text(
        face = "bold",
        angle = 0,
        size = strip_size,
        margin = ggplot2::margin(r = 8)),
      strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(
        face = "bold",
        size = strip_size),
      strip.placement = "outside",
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size + 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(size = base_size - 0.5),
      plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(6, 8, 6, 8))
}

#FS5.4 Build Panel A: contrasts versus no diagnosis
figS5_A_df <- figS5_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(panel_label == "A. Contrasts versus no diagnosis")

p_figS5_A <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  figS5_A_df,
  ggplot2::aes(x = or, y = wave_label)) +
  ggplot2::geom_vline(
    xintercept = 1,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.4,
    color = "grey40") +
  ggplot2::geom_segment(
    ggplot2::aes(x = or_lcl, xend = or_ucl, yend = wave_label),
    linewidth = 0.4,
    color = "black") +
  ggplot2::geom_point(
    size = 2.2,
    color = "#7F3C8D") +
  ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, fs5_xmax),
    breaks = fs5_x_breaks,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.01))) +
  ggplot2::facet_grid(
    outcome_label ~ .,
    switch = "y",
    labeller = ggplot2::label_wrap_gen(width = 30)) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "A. Contrasts versus no diagnosis",
    x = NULL) +
  forest_theme_s5() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = ggplot2::element_blank())

#FS5.5 Build Panel B: direct pairwise comparison
figS5_B_df <- figS5_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(panel_label == "B. Direct pairwise comparison")

p_figS5_B <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  figS5_B_df,
  ggplot2::aes(x = or, y = wave_label)) +
  ggplot2::geom_vline(
    xintercept = 1,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.4,
    color = "grey40") +
  ggplot2::geom_segment(
    ggplot2::aes(x = or_lcl, xend = or_ucl, yend = wave_label),
    linewidth = 0.4,
    color = "black") +
  ggplot2::geom_point(
    size = 2.2,
    color = "#7F3C8D") +
  ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, fs5_xmax),
    breaks = fs5_x_breaks,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.01))) +
  ggplot2::facet_grid(
    outcome_label ~ .,
    switch = "y",
    labeller = ggplot2::label_wrap_gen(width = 30)) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "B. Direct pairwise comparison",
    x = "Odds ratio (Higher-Risk vs Lower-Risk)") +
  forest_theme_s5()

#FS5.6 Combine into final Figure S5
figS5 <- p_figS5_A / p_figS5_B +
  patchwork::plot_layout(heights = c(2.2, 1.15)) +
  patchwork::plot_annotation(
    title = "Figure S5. Common-comorbidity sensitivity-analysis odds ratios by wave",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Odds ratios compare Higher-Risk versus Lower-Risk cluster membership across the diagnostic-group contrasts ",
      "reported in the supplementary results. Horizontal lines indicate 95% confidence intervals; the dashed ",
      "vertical line marks the null value (OR = 1)."),
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
      plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(size = 10)))

#FS5.7 Save and print Figure S5
save_fig(figS5, "Figure_S5_Common_Comorbidity_Sensitivity_ORs", width = 8.8, height = 7.4)
figS5

```

## Utility of Alternative Baseline Risk Representations Sensitivity-Analysis Inputs

- Purpose: Use the saved comparison-summary and coefficient tables from the alternative baseline-risk representation sensitivity-analysis script
- Inputs: `utility_takeaway`, `dth_utility_coef`, and `prev_utility_coef`
- Outputs: In-memory objects passed directly to Table S7, Table S8, Table S9, and optional Figure S6
- Notes: These objects compare 3 competing baseline-risk representations on identical analytic rows complete for the full 17-variable baseline predictor block, consistent with the supplementary methods

## Table S7 - Relative downstream BSD-related information retained by alternative baseline risk representations in first-onset and occurrence models

- Purpose: Summarize relative downstream information retained by the cluster-label, MVFS, and full 17-variable baseline representations across first-onset and occurrence BSD models
- Inputs: `utility_takeaway`
- Outputs: Saves `Table_S7_Alternative_Baseline_Risk_Representation_Utility` as HTML/PNG/RTF in `results/tables`; returns a gt table
- Notes: Primary metrics are McFadden pseudo-R2 for first-onset models and row-level in-sample AUC for occurrence models; joint block-test p-values correspond to the MVFS and full 17-variable predictor blocks beyond shared covariates

```{r table s7}

## Table S7: Relative downstream BSD-related information retained by alternative baseline risk representations ##

#S7.1 Guard against placeholder input
if (is_tk(utility_takeaway)) {
  stop(
    "Table S7 requires results/main_analysis/2_statistical_analysis/6_risk_vars_vs_cluster_utility/tables/cluster_vs_mvfs_vs_raw_takeaway_table.csv. ",
    "Run 6_risk_vars_vs_cluster_utility.Rmd first, then knit again.")
}

#S7.2 Helper functions
fmt_p_s7 <- function(p) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(p) ~ "\u2014",
    p < 0.001 ~ "<.001",
    TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", round(p, 3)))
}

#S7.3 Prepare publication-ready Table S7 data
tS7_df <- utility_takeaway %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    domain = dplyr::recode(
      domain,
      "DTH" = "Onset",
      "Prevalence" = "Prevalence"),
    outcome = factor(
      outcome,
      levels = c("BD-I", "BD-II", "BD-NOS", "Any BSD")),
    domain = factor(
      domain,
      levels = c("Onset", "Prevalence"))) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(domain, outcome) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    `Model Type` = domain,
    `Outcome` = outcome,
    `Primary Metric Type` = primary_metric,
    `Cluster-Label Model Primary Metric Value` = as.numeric(cluster_primary),
    `MVFS 7-Variable Model Primary Metric Value` = as.numeric(mvfs_primary),
    `Full 17-Variable Model Primary Metric Value` = as.numeric(raw_primary),
    `Secondary Metric Type` = secondary_metric,
    `Cluster-Label Model Secondary Metric Value` = as.numeric(cluster_secondary),
    `MVFS 7-Variable Model Secondary Metric Value` = as.numeric(mvfs_secondary),
    `Full 17-Variable Model Secondary Metric Value` = as.numeric(raw_secondary),
    `MVFS 7-Variable Model Block Test P-Value` = fmt_p_s7(mvfs_block_p),
    `Full 17-Variable Model Block Test P-Value` = fmt_p_s7(raw_block_p))

#S7.4 Create caption
tS7_caption <- paste0(
  "**Table S7. Relative downstream BSD-related information retained by alternative baseline risk representations in first-onset and occurrence models**  \n",
  "All 3 baseline-risk representations were compared on identical analytic rows complete for the full 17-variable baseline predictor block. ",
  "Primary descriptive metrics were McFadden pseudo-R2 for first-onset models and row-level in-sample AUC for occurrence models.")

#S7.5 Build gt table
tS7_gt <- gt::gt(tS7_df) |>
  gt::fmt_number(
    columns = c(
      `Cluster-Label Model Primary Metric Value`,
      `MVFS 7-Variable Model Primary Metric Value`,
      `Full 17-Variable Model Primary Metric Value`,
      `Cluster-Label Model Secondary Metric Value`,
      `MVFS 7-Variable Model Secondary Metric Value`,
      `Full 17-Variable Model Secondary Metric Value`),
    decimals = 3) |>
  gt::cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = dplyr::everything()) |>
  gt::tab_caption(tS7_caption) |>
  gt::tab_source_note(
    gt::md(paste(
      "For onset models, the secondary metric is the Brier score; for prevalence models, the secondary metric is log loss.",
      "Block-test p-values correspond to joint Wald tests for the MVFS or full 17-variable baseline predictor block beyond shared covariates.",
      "Occurrence-model AUCs are descriptive row-level in-sample summaries and should not be interpreted as internally validated subject-level predictive performance.",
      sep = "  \n"))) |>
  gt::tab_options(table.font.size = gt::px(12))

#S7.6 Save and print Table S7
save_gt(tS7_gt, "Table_S7_Alternative_Baseline_Risk_Representation_Utility")
tS7_gt

```

## Table S8 - Participant-clustered robust-SE adjusted odds ratios for baseline risk representations in discrete-time hazard models of first-onset bipolar spectrum outcomes

- Purpose: Reproduce the substantive predictor rows from the alternative baseline-risk representation discrete-time hazard models
- Inputs: `dth_utility_coef`
- Outputs: Saves `Table_S8_DTH_Alternative_Baseline_Risk_Representation_ORs` as HTML/PNG/RTF in `results/tables`; returns a gt table
- Notes: Displays only the baseline-risk representation terms of interest; nuisance covariates such as sex, wave, age, and intercept terms are omitted for readability

```{r table s8}

## Table S8: Participant-clustered robust-SE adjusted odds ratios for first-onset models ##

#S8.1 Guard against placeholder input
if (is_tk(dth_utility_coef)) {
  stop(
    "Table S8 requires results/main_analysis/2_statistical_analysis/6_risk_vars_vs_cluster_utility/tables/dth_all_model_robust_coefficients.csv. ",
    "Run 6_risk_vars_vs_cluster_utility.Rmd first, then knit again.")
}

#S8.2 Helper functions and ordering objects
utility_outcome_map <- c(
  "bipolar_I" = "BD-I",
  "bipolar_II" = "BD-II",
  "bd_nos" = "BD-NOS",
  "any_bsd" = "Any BSD")

utility_model_map <- c(
  "cluster_model" = "Cluster Label Model",
  "mvfs_model" = "MVFS 7-Variable Model",
  "raw_model" = "Full 17-Variable Model")

utility_term_map <- c(
  "clusterC2" = "Risk Cluster",
  "mh_p_cbcl__dsm__dep_tscore" = "CBCL DSM-5 Depression T",
  "mh_p_cbcl__dsm__anx_tscore" = "CBCL DSM-5 Anxiety T",
  "mh_p_cbcl__synd__attn_tscore" = "CBCL Attention Problems T",
  "mh_p_cbcl__synd__aggr_tscore" = "CBCL Aggression Problems T",
  "mh_p_gbi_sum" = "GBI Mania Sum Score",
  "sds_total" = "SDSC Sum Score",
  "family_history_depression1" = "Family History of Depression",
  "mh_y_upps__nurg_sum" = "UPPS-P Negative Urgency Score",
  "mh_y_upps__purg_sum" = "UPPS-P Positive Urgency Score",
  "le_l_coi__addr1__coi__total__national_zscore" = "COI Z-Score",
  "fc_p_nsc__ns_mean" = "Mean Neighborhood Safety",
  "family_history_mania1" = "Family History of Mania",
  "bullying1" = "Bullying Victimization",
  "nc_y_nihtb__lswmt__uncor_score" = "NIHTB List Sorting Score",
  "nc_y_nihtb__flnkr__uncor_score" = "NIHTB Flanker Score",
```
