```{r table s8}
  "nc_y_nihtb__pttcp__uncor_score" = "NIHTB Pattern Comparison Score",
  "ACE_index_sum_score" = "ACE Index Sum Score")

mvfs_term_order_s8 <- c(
  "mh_p_cbcl__dsm__dep_tscore",
  "mh_p_cbcl__synd__aggr_tscore",
  "mh_p_cbcl__synd__attn_tscore",
  "mh_p_cbcl__dsm__anx_tscore",
  "mh_p_gbi_sum",
  "sds_total",
  "family_history_depression1")

raw_term_order_s8 <- c(
  "mh_p_cbcl__dsm__dep_tscore",
  "mh_p_cbcl__dsm__anx_tscore",
  "mh_p_cbcl__synd__attn_tscore",
  "mh_p_cbcl__synd__aggr_tscore",
  "mh_p_gbi_sum",
  "mh_y_upps__nurg_sum",
  "mh_y_upps__purg_sum",
  "le_l_coi__addr1__coi__total__national_zscore",
  "fc_p_nsc__ns_mean",
  "sds_total",
  "family_history_depression1",
  "family_history_mania1",
  "bullying1",
  "nc_y_nihtb__lswmt__uncor_score",
  "nc_y_nihtb__flnkr__uncor_score",
  "nc_y_nihtb__pttcp__uncor_score",
  "ACE_index_sum_score")

fmt_or_ci_s8 <- function(or, lo, hi) {
  sprintf("%.3f [%.3f, %.3f]", or, lo, hi)
}

fmt_p_s8 <- function(p) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(p) ~ "\u2014",
    p < 0.001 ~ "<.001",
    TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", round(p, 3)))
}

#S8.3 Prepare publication-ready Table S8 data
tS8_df <- dth_utility_coef %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    (model == "cluster_model" & term == "clusterc2") |
      (model == "mvfs_model" & term %in% tolower(mvfs_term_order_s8)) |
      (model == "raw_model" & term %in% tolower(raw_term_order_s8))) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    term = dplyr::case_when(
      term == "clusterc2" ~ "clusterC2",
      term == "family_history_depression1" ~ "family_history_depression1",
      term == "family_history_mania1" ~ "family_history_mania1",
      term == "bullying1" ~ "bullying1",
      TRUE ~ term),
    outcome_label = dplyr::recode(outcome, !!!utility_outcome_map),
    model_label = dplyr::recode(model, !!!utility_model_map),
    term_label = dplyr::recode(term, !!!utility_term_map),
    outcome_order = match(outcome, c("bipolar_I", "bipolar_II", "bd_nos", "any_bsd")),
    model_order = match(model, c("cluster_model", "mvfs_model", "raw_model")),
    term_order = dplyr::case_when(
      model == "cluster_model" ~ 1L,
      model == "mvfs_model" ~ match(term, mvfs_term_order_s8),
      model == "raw_model" ~ match(term, raw_term_order_s8),
      TRUE ~ 999L)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(outcome_order, model_order, term_order) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    `Outcome` = outcome_label,
    `Model` = model_label,
    `Variable` = term_label,
    `SE Type` = se_type,
    `SE` = as.numeric(se),
    `OR [95% CI]` = fmt_or_ci_s8(as.numeric(or), as.numeric(ci_lo), as.numeric(ci_hi)),
    `P-Value` = fmt_p_s8(p_value))

#S8.4 Create caption
tS8_caption <- paste0(
  "**Table S8. Participant-clustered robust-SE adjusted odds ratios for baseline risk representations in discrete-time hazard models of first-onset bipolar spectrum outcomes**  \n",
  "Only baseline-risk representation terms are shown. Shared adjustment covariates were sex assigned at birth, interval indicator, and between-person age.")

#S8.5 Build gt table
tS8_gt <- gt::gt(tS8_df) |>
  gt::fmt_number(
    columns = `SE`,
    decimals = 3) |>
  gt::cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(`Outcome`, `Model`, `Variable`, `SE Type`, `SE`, `OR [95% CI]`, `P-Value`)) |>
  gt::tab_caption(tS8_caption) |>
  gt::tab_source_note(
    gt::md(paste(
      "Cluster-label rows use participant-clustered CR2 robust standard errors.",
      "MVFS and full 17-variable rows use participant-clustered HC1 robust standard errors when CR2 was not estimable in the higher-parameter specifications.",
      sep = "  \n"))) |>
  gt::tab_options(table.font.size = gt::px(12))

#S8.6 Save and print Table S8
save_gt(tS8_gt, "Table_S8_DTH_Alternative_Baseline_Risk_Representation_ORs")
tS8_gt

```

## Table S9 - Robust-SE adjusted odds ratios for baseline risk representations in generalized estimating equation models of bipolar spectrum outcome occurrence

- Purpose: Reproduce the substantive predictor rows from the alternative baseline-risk representation GEE occurrence models
- Inputs: `prev_utility_coef`
- Outputs: Saves `Table_S9_GEE_Alternative_Baseline_Risk_Representation_ORs` as HTML/PNG/RTF in `results/tables`; returns a gt table
- Notes: Displays only the baseline-risk representation terms of interest; nuisance covariates such as wave, sex, baseline outcome status, age, and intercept terms are omitted for readability

```{r table s9}

## Table S9: Robust-SE adjusted odds ratios for occurrence models ##

#S9.1 Guard against placeholder input
if (is_tk(prev_utility_coef)) {
  stop(
    "Table S9 requires results/main_analysis/2_statistical_analysis/6_risk_vars_vs_cluster_utility/tables/prevalence_all_model_coefficients.csv. ",
    "Run 6_risk_vars_vs_cluster_utility.Rmd first, then knit again.")
}

#S9.2 Helper functions
fmt_or_ci_s9 <- function(or, lo, hi) {
  sprintf("%.3f [%.3f, %.3f]", or, lo, hi)
}

fmt_p_s9 <- function(p) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(p) ~ "\u2014",
    p < 0.001 ~ "<.001",
    TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", round(p, 3)))
}

#S9.3 Prepare publication-ready Table S9 data
tS9_df <- prev_utility_coef %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    (model == "cluster_model" & term == "clusterc2") |
      (model == "mvfs_model" & term %in% tolower(mvfs_term_order_s8)) |
      (model == "raw_model" & term %in% tolower(raw_term_order_s8))) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    term = dplyr::case_when(
      term == "clusterc2" ~ "clusterC2",
      term == "family_history_depression1" ~ "family_history_depression1",
      term == "family_history_mania1" ~ "family_history_mania1",
      term == "bullying1" ~ "bullying1",
      TRUE ~ term),
    outcome_label = dplyr::recode(outcome, !!!utility_outcome_map),
    model_label = dplyr::recode(model, !!!utility_model_map),
    term_label = dplyr::recode(term, !!!utility_term_map),
    outcome_order = match(outcome, c("bipolar_I", "bipolar_II", "bd_nos", "any_bsd")),
    model_order = match(model, c("cluster_model", "mvfs_model", "raw_model")),
    term_order = dplyr::case_when(
      model == "cluster_model" ~ 1L,
      model == "mvfs_model" ~ match(term, mvfs_term_order_s8),
      model == "raw_model" ~ match(term, raw_term_order_s8),
      TRUE ~ 999L)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(outcome_order, model_order, term_order) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    `Outcome` = outcome_label,
    `Model` = model_label,
    `Variable` = term_label,
    `SE` = as.numeric(se),
    `OR [95% CI]` = fmt_or_ci_s9(as.numeric(or), as.numeric(ci_lo), as.numeric(ci_hi)),
    `P-Value` = fmt_p_s9(p_value))

#S9.4 Create caption
tS9_caption <- paste0(
  "**Table S9. Robust-SE adjusted odds ratios for baseline risk representations in generalized estimating equation models of bipolar spectrum outcome occurrence**  \n",
  "Only baseline-risk representation terms are shown. Shared adjustment covariates were wave, sex assigned at birth, baseline outcome status, and grand-mean centered age.")

#S9.5 Build gt table
tS9_gt <- gt::gt(tS9_df) |>
  gt::fmt_number(
    columns = `SE`,
    decimals = 3) |>
  gt::cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(`Outcome`, `Model`, `Variable`, `SE`, `OR [95% CI]`, `P-Value`)) |>
  gt::tab_caption(tS9_caption) |>
  gt::tab_source_note(
    gt::md(
      "Standard errors are the robust sandwich standard errors returned by the generalized estimating equation models.")) |>
  gt::tab_options(table.font.size = gt::px(12))

#S9.6 Save and print Table S9
save_gt(tS9_gt, "Table_S9_GEE_Alternative_Baseline_Risk_Representation_ORs")
tS9_gt

```

## Figure S6 - Cluster-label effect magnitudes versus strongest single-predictor effect magnitudes across alternative baseline risk representations

- Purpose: Distill the main interpretive insight from the alternative baseline-risk representation sensitivity analysis by comparing the risk-cluster effect size with the strongest single-predictor effect size from the MVFS and full 17-variable models
- Inputs: `dth_utility_coef` and `prev_utility_coef`
- Outputs: Saves `Figure_S6_Cluster_vs_Strongest_Single_Predictor_Effect_Magnitudes.{pdf,png}` in results/figures; returns a ggplot object
- Notes: For the MVFS and full 17-variable models, the plotted point is the single baseline predictor with the largest odds-ratio magnitude relative to 1.0 within that outcome/model, defined as exp(abs(log(OR))); inferential details remain in Tables S8 and S9

```{r figure s6}

## Figure S6: Cluster-label versus strongest single-predictor ORs across alternative baseline risk representations ##

#FS6.1 Guard against placeholder inputs
if (is_tk(dth_utility_coef) || is_tk(prev_utility_coef)) {
  stop(
    "Figure S6 requires results/main_analysis/2_statistical_analysis/6_risk_vars_vs_cluster_utility/tables/",
    "dth_all_model_robust_coefficients.csv and prevalence_all_model_coefficients.csv. ",
    "Run 6_risk_vars_vs_cluster_utility.Rmd first, then knit again.")
}

#FS6.2 Define pretty-label maps
utility_outcome_map_s6 <- c(
  "bipolar_I" = "BD-I",
  "bipolar_II" = "BD-II",
  "bd_nos" = "BD-NOS",
  "any_bsd" = "Any BSD")

utility_term_map_s6 <- c(
  "clusterc2" = "Risk Cluster",
  "mh_p_cbcl__dsm__dep_tscore" = "CBCL DSM-5 Depression T",
  "mh_p_cbcl__dsm__anx_tscore" = "CBCL DSM-5 Anxiety T",
  "mh_p_cbcl__synd__attn_tscore" = "CBCL Attention Problems T",
  "mh_p_cbcl__synd__aggr_tscore" = "CBCL Aggression Problems T",
  "mh_p_gbi_sum" = "GBI Mania Sum Score",
  "sds_total" = "SDSC Sum Score",
  "family_history_depression1" = "Family History of Depression",
  "mh_y_upps__nurg_sum" = "UPPS-P Negative Urgency Score",
  "mh_y_upps__purg_sum" = "UPPS-P Positive Urgency Score",
  "le_l_coi__addr1__coi__total__national_zscore" = "COI Z-Score",
  "fc_p_nsc__ns_mean" = "Mean Neighborhood Safety",
  "family_history_mania1" = "Family History of Mania",
  "bullying1" = "Bullying Victimization",
  "nc_y_nihtb__lswmt__uncor_score" = "NIHTB List Sorting Score",
  "nc_y_nihtb__flnkr__uncor_score" = "NIHTB Flanker Score",
  "nc_y_nihtb__pttcp__uncor_score" = "NIHTB Pattern Comparison Score",
  "ace_index_sum_score" = "ACE Index Sum Score")

#FS6.3 Define the baseline predictor sets
mvfs_terms_s6 <- c(
  "mh_p_cbcl__dsm__dep_tscore",
  "mh_p_cbcl__synd__aggr_tscore",
  "mh_p_cbcl__synd__attn_tscore",
  "mh_p_cbcl__dsm__anx_tscore",
  "mh_p_gbi_sum",
  "sds_total",
  "family_history_depression1")

raw_terms_s6 <- c(
  "mh_p_cbcl__dsm__dep_tscore",
  "mh_p_cbcl__dsm__anx_tscore",
  "mh_p_cbcl__synd__attn_tscore",
  "mh_p_cbcl__synd__aggr_tscore",
  "mh_p_gbi_sum",
  "mh_y_upps__nurg_sum",
  "mh_y_upps__purg_sum",
  "le_l_coi__addr1__coi__total__national_zscore",
  "fc_p_nsc__ns_mean",
  "sds_total",
  "family_history_depression1",
  "family_history_mania1",
  "bullying1",
  "nc_y_nihtb__lswmt__uncor_score",
  "nc_y_nihtb__flnkr__uncor_score",
  "nc_y_nihtb__pttcp__uncor_score",
  "ace_index_sum_score")

#FS6.4 Create helpers to standardize coefficient tables and normalize keys
norm_key_s6 <- function(x) {
  stringr::str_replace_all(
    stringr::str_to_lower(as.character(x)),
    "[^a-z0-9]+",
    "")
}

mvfs_terms_key_s6 <- norm_key_s6(mvfs_terms_s6)
raw_terms_key_s6  <- norm_key_s6(raw_terms_s6)

prep_coef_s6 <- function(df, domain_label) {
  df <- df %>% janitor::clean_names()

  if (!"or" %in% names(df) && "estimate" %in% names(df)) {
    df <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(or = exp(estimate))
  }

  if (!"ci_lo" %in% names(df)) {
    if ("conf_low" %in% names(df)) {
      df <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(ci_lo = conf_low)
    } else if ("ci_low" %in% names(df)) {
      df <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(ci_lo = ci_low)
    } else {
      df <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(ci_lo = NA_real_)
    }
  }

  if (!"ci_hi" %in% names(df)) {
    if ("conf_high" %in% names(df)) {
      df <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(ci_hi = conf_high)
    } else if ("ci_high" %in% names(df)) {
      df <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(ci_hi = ci_high)
    } else {
      df <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(ci_hi = NA_real_)
    }
  }

  df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      domain_label = domain_label,
      model_key = norm_key_s6(model),
      term_key = norm_key_s6(term),
      outcome_label = dplyr::recode(outcome, !!!utility_outcome_map_s6, .default = outcome),
      term_label = dplyr::recode(term, !!!utility_term_map_s6, .default = term),
      or = as.numeric(or),
      ci_lo = as.numeric(ci_lo),
      ci_hi = as.numeric(ci_hi),
      effect_abs_log = abs(log(or)))
}

#FS6.5 Extract the cluster effect and strongest single predictor per outcome/model
extract_top_effects_s6 <- function(df, domain_label) {

  df0 <- prep_coef_s6(df, domain_label)

  #FS6.5.1 Keep the cluster-label model row
  clus_df <- df0 %>%
    dplyr::filter(
      stringr::str_detect(model_key, "cluster"),
      term_key %in% c("clusterc2", "riskcluster")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(outcome_label) %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 1) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      display_group = "Risk Cluster",
      y_pos = 3,
      row_label = "Risk Cluster")

  #FS6.5.2 Keep the strongest MVFS predictor by absolute log-OR magnitude
  mvfs_df <- df0 %>%
    dplyr::filter(
      stringr::str_detect(model_key, "mvfs"),
      term_key %in% mvfs_terms_key_s6) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(outcome_label) %>%
    dplyr::slice_max(order_by = effect_abs_log, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      display_group = "Strongest MVFS Predictor",
      y_pos = 2,
      row_label = paste0(term_label))

  #FS6.5.3 Keep the strongest full-model predictor by absolute log-OR magnitude
  raw_df <- df0 %>%
    dplyr::filter(
      (stringr::str_detect(model_key, "17") |
         stringr::str_detect(model_key, "raw") |
         stringr::str_detect(model_key, "full")) &
        !stringr::str_detect(model_key, "mvfs") &
        !stringr::str_detect(model_key, "cluster"),
      term_key %in% raw_terms_key_s6) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(outcome_label) %>%
    dplyr::slice_max(order_by = effect_abs_log, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      display_group = "Strongest Full-Model Predictor",
      y_pos = 1,
      row_label = paste0(term_label))

  dplyr::bind_rows(clus_df, mvfs_df, raw_df) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      row_label_plot = stringr::str_wrap(row_label, width = 16),
      display_group = factor(
        display_group,
        levels = c(
          "Risk Cluster",
          "Strongest MVFS Predictor",
          "Strongest Full-Model Predictor")),
      outcome_label = factor(
        outcome_label,
        levels = c("BD-I", "BD-II", "BD-NOS", "Any BSD")))
}

#FS6.6 Build plotting data
figS6_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  extract_top_effects_s6(
    dth_utility_coef,
    "A. First-onset models"),
  extract_top_effects_s6(
    prev_utility_coef,
    "B. Occurrence models"))

#FS6.6.1 Save source data for auditing
readr::write_csv(
  figS6_df %>%
    dplyr::select(
      domain_label,
      outcome_label,
      display_group,
      row_label,
      term,
      term_label,
      or,
      ci_lo,
      ci_hi),
  file.path(tab_dir, "Figure_S6_Cluster_vs_Strongest_Predictor_ORs_SourceData.csv"))

#FS6.7 Set figure bounds and display helpers
fs6_xmax <- max(figS6_df$ci_hi, na.rm = TRUE)
fs6_xmax <- ceiling(fs6_xmax / 0.5) * 0.5
fs6_xmax <- max(fs6_xmax, 5)

#FS6.7.1 Create point-adjacent labels positioned slightly above/right of each OR
fs6_label_dx <- 0.15
fs6_label_xmax_buffer <- 0.50

figS6_df <- figS6_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    row_label_plot = stringr::str_wrap(row_label, width = 16),
    label_x = pmin(or + fs6_label_dx, fs6_xmax - fs6_label_xmax_buffer),
    label_y = y_pos + dplyr::case_when(
      y_pos == 3 ~ 0.18,
      y_pos == 2 ~ 0.15,
      y_pos == 1 ~ 0.18,
      TRUE ~ 0.16))

fs6_cols <- c(
  "Risk Cluster" = "#7F3C8D",
  "Strongest MVFS Predictor" = "#11A579",
  "Strongest Full-Model Predictor" = "#3969AC")

fig_theme_s6 <- function(base_size = 16) {
  ggplot2::theme_classic(base_size = base_size) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      strip.background = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size + 0.5),
      axis.title.y = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.y = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      axis.title.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = base_size),
      axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = base_size - 0.5),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      legend.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = base_size - 0.5),
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size + 1.6),
      plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(size = base_size - 0.1),
      panel.spacing.x = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
      panel.spacing.y = grid::unit(1.0, "lines"),
      plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(10, 14, 10, 14))
}

#FS6.8 Build the figure
figS6 <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  figS6_df,
  ggplot2::aes(
    x = or,
    y = y_pos,
    color = display_group)) +
  ggplot2::geom_vline(
    xintercept = 1,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    color = "grey45") +
  ggplot2::geom_segment(
    ggplot2::aes(x = ci_lo, xend = ci_hi, yend = y_pos),
    linewidth = 0.7) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(
    size = 2.8) +
  ggplot2::geom_label(
    data = figS6_df,
    ggplot2::aes(
      x = label_x,
      y = label_y,
      label = row_label_plot),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 0,
    lineheight = 0.90,
    size = 4.0,
    color = "black",
    fill = "white",
    label.size = NA,
    label.padding = grid::unit(0.08, "lines")) +
  ggplot2::facet_grid(
    domain_label ~ outcome_label) +
  ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, fs6_xmax, by = 1),
    expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.03))) +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0.4, 3.75),
    breaks = c(1, 2, 3),
    labels = NULL) +
  ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
    values = fs6_cols,
    breaks = names(fs6_cols),
    labels = c(
      "Risk Cluster",
      "Strongest MVFS Predictor",
      "Strongest Full-Model Predictor")) +
  ggplot2::coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, fs6_xmax),
    clip = "on") +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Figure S6. Cluster-label odds ratios versus strongest single-predictor odds ratios",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Within each outcome, the adjusted higher- versus lower-risk cluster odds ratio is shown alongside the single ",
      "baseline predictor with the largest absolute log-OR magnitude from the MVFS and full 17-variable models. ",
      "Horizontal bars denote 95% confidence intervals."),
    x = "Odds Ratio") +
  fig_theme_s6()

#FS6.9 Save and print Figure S6
save_fig(figS6, "Figure_S6_Cluster_vs_Strongest_Predictor_ORs", width = 14.75, height = 9.75, dpi = 1440)

figS6

```
